review:
I used to love this game and have spent countless nights where I should have been studying, ruling the star systems with friends. However I am so tired of taking a break, coming back and having to relearn the game because they rework how the game runs and functions every month or so. Very few times have these changes actually improved the game, many break it. 

I understand it's paradox, but the DLC are insane, I have most of them and can that most of them should have been built into the main game for free. 

Last but not least.. The User data collection and selling of such data to third parties is such a low blow, especially for a company that charges you $40 for a base game and $160 for DLC afterwards. The data collection is invasive, unnecessary, and downright greedy. 

If I could, I wish I could return the game, DLC, and get my life back that I wasted with this game

review:
The game is currently rated very positive for all time reviews, and mixed for recent reviews. Let me explain what's happening.

Game good. Game get update. Update causes loads of lag and very bug-ridden. Fanbase justifiably upset. Mixed reviews.

That being said, they have been working on addressing these issues since and the game runs better now. Stellaris has recently hit its 10-year anniversary and with this game getting constant free updates and new content since I think that while I agree the production team isn't without criticism the game is still really, really high quality.

review: Like all paradox games, this is a scam meant to sell you DLC. The base game is a hollow shell. I paid for 1 month subscription to all the DLC and I can tell you that none of it is worth your cold hard cash. They sell you almost nothing in each DLC. It only looks like a lot of content spread out over 10 years of bloated development. They still demand retail prices for new DLC and season passes. The next DLC they promise ships that function like planets. Sounds cool but not worth paying money for it. If Paradox was a good company, they would have stopped developing this game and made Stellaris 2. But the quality in their games have spiraled into the gutter and all they care about is milking DLC. All the veterans are mad about changes and it's clear the game's balance is ruined with each new patch. They censor forums, ban dissenting opinions, and have armies of shills that will berate you for any criticism. Once you have played a complete game or two, everything becomes boring and the events are no longer interesting.

review:
我没能等到人类驶向深空，但群星让我提前赴了这场约。

在我眼里，银河从不是用来征服的，是用来填补遗憾的。探索、飞升、天灾、外交——每一局都像在另一条时间线上，亲手为文明点亮一片星海。生得太早，触不到真正的星辰。

但至少在这里，我决定过它们如何闪耀。

review:
Viele Jahre war Stellaris mein bevorzugtes Strategie-Spiel. Leider hat es sich in eine Richtung entwickelt die nichts mehr für mich ist. 

Ich möchte die Galaxie erobern und dabei nicht von stark erhöhten Mali durch Reichsgröße eingeschränkt und zu Unmengen an Micromanagement gezwungen werden. Der Einfluß von Reichsgröße auf Aufstände ist außerdem extrem lästig und es gibt keine wirksamen Mittel im Spiel, um diese Niederzugschlagen oder aufzulösen - man muß also jedes Mal die betroffenen Systeme zurückerobern -  mitten in Krisen mehr als nur lästig. Das ganze Balanceing paßt einfach nicht mehr. Ich möchte nicht die meisten Gebiete durch Vasallen kontrollieren, sondern sie selber besetzen und halten. Das ist ab einer gewissen Größe quasi nicht mehr sinnvoll möglich. Außerdem sind die gefallenen Reiche, die gegeneinander Krieg führen, heute unverhältnismäßig stark - und sie schalten sich nicht mehr gegenseitig aus, so daß die Flottengrößengrenzen selbst bei optimal bis dahin verlaufenen Spiel nicht ausreichend Gegenwehr zulassen, nicht einmal mit großen Föderationsflotten. Alles in allem macht das Spiel im gegenwärtigen Zustand keinen Spaß mehr, da es früher einfach erheblich ausgewogener war. Die gewachsene Komplexität wurde in den falschen Bereichen geschaffen. Anstatt Wirtschaft, Planeten und Spionage umfangreicher zu machen, hätte ich mir gewünscht, die Komplexität im Bereich Flotten und Kriegsführung zu erhöhen und den Malus auf Reichsgröße abzuschaffen.

review_english: For many years, Stellaris was my favorite strategy game. Unfortunately it has developed in a direction that is no longer for me. I want to conquer the galaxy and not be limited by greatly increased penalties due to empire size and forced to do tons of micromanagement. The influence of empire size on uprisings is also extremely annoying and there are no effective means in the game to crush or resolve them - so you have to retake the affected systems every time - more than just annoying in the middle of crises. The whole balancing thing just doesn't work anymore. I don't want to control most areas through vassals, but rather occupy and hold them myself. Once you reach a certain size, this is virtually no longer possible. In addition, the fallen empires that are waging war against each other are now disproportionately strong - and they no longer eliminate each other, so that the fleet size limits do not allow for sufficient resistance, even if the game has been going optimally up to that point, not even with large Federation fleets. All in all, the game is no longer fun in its current state, as it used to be much more balanced. The increased complexity was created in the wrong areas. Instead of making the economy, planets and espionage more extensive, I would have liked to have increased the complexity of fleets and warfare and removed the empire size penalty.

This has been the first time i uninstalled the game since i've bought it. I don't know what Paradox is thinking, or if they are thinking at all, but at this point, every single time they touch their game, it just gets worse and worse and worse, and it has frankly become unbearable.
The constant nerfs, the constant rehauling of things that work into things that barely work... it is too much.

Paradox. Just stop. Make a Stellaris 2. Leave Stellaris 1 alone. 
When people bought this game, they weren't signing up for a live service game with yearly rehauls of all of it's systems.

review:
Good game, BUT !

+ + + A very good simulation of diplomacy, empire building, resource and population management and a bit of spaceship combat as well. Was lots of fun building a galactic empire with my little brother together. Overall a good strategy game. 

- - - Note that 70% of the content what this game can to offer is locked behind DLC paywalls. OVER 100€ WORTH OF DLC ON SALE.... Lates DLC is 25€... Alternative option is the monthly sub for a 10 years old game. 
Too much greed and not enough focus on the development on the gameplay.

One comment I was reading in a forum and completely agree with: 
""I have the impression that the Paradox game developers are under a strong constant pressure to add new features into the game, so that Paradox can sell more DLC. Due to this, Paradox games are constantly being flooded with large amounts of half-baked new features, which is hurting the core gameplay of its games. I really wish that Paradox would spend more time polishing existing features and fixing bugs, and would be more careful about adding new features to the game, because these new features often upset the delicate balance of the game.""

- Get the base game on sale and if you like it then add some DLC you like.
But be aware, this game is designed that the more you play the more DLC content you wanna see.

review:
Игра отличная, увлекательная и интересная... но только на старых версиях. Не рекомендую играть в новые версии Стеллариса, можете попробовать через бета-версии поискать ту, которая вам будет интересна и просто накатывать на неё моды и всё. Постоянные дополнения и обновы это просто пыль в глаза, Парадоксы ничего не добавляют в игру, а только меняют старые механики, уничтожая их смысл. По моему мнению, после плиток игру начали понемногу убивать. Вот представьте себе, играть на версии 8 летней давности интереснее, чем в новые с кучей дополнений, это уже о многом говорит.
Я рекомендую версию 2.1.4, да старая, да плитки, но вы офигеете насколько комфортно играть, ведь вам не нужно прокликивать 10000 кнопок за секунду, не нужно ставить каждую миллисекунду перки лидерам, нет ивентов, которые вылетает каждые 5 секунд и тупой системы раскопок... просто обычная игра, с нормальным количеством ивентов, с нормальной механикой лидеров, пусть и не глубокой, в конце концов с НОРМАЛЬНЫМ балансом экономики и боя. И самое главное, вы насладитесь игрой, послушаете музыку, подумаете о жизни, а не будете каждые пол секунды прокликивать бесполезную хрень.

review_english: The game is excellent, exciting and interesting... but only on older versions. I don’t recommend playing new versions of Stellaris, you can try using beta versions to look for one that will be interesting to you and just roll mods on it and that’s it. Constant additions and updates are just dust in the eyes. Paradoxes do not add anything to the game, but only change old mechanics, destroying their meaning. In my opinion, after the tiles the game began to be killed little by little. Just imagine, playing the version from 8 years ago is more interesting than playing new ones with a bunch of additions, this already says a lot. I recommend version 2.1.4, yes old, yes tiles, but you will be amazed how comfortable it is to play, because you don’t need to click 10,000 buttons per second, you don’t need to put perks on the leaders every millisecond, there are no events that crash every 5 seconds and a stupid excavation system... just an ordinary game, with a normal number of events, with normal leader mechanics, albeit not deep, in the end with NORMAL balance of economy and combat. And most importantly, you will enjoy the game, listen to music, think about life, and not click through useless crap every half second.

review:
Sadly, after so long of really loving and enjoying this game, I am gonna have to write this. (I have played the game since Hyperlanes were an option, not the given)

It really is a shell of its former self in terms of what it feels like to play, 5 years ago, every game you had felt unique and different, and this is with there being LESS content then there currently is.

This is because that, despite the many times more content in the game now then before, the quality has been dropping farther and farther. Each big update leaves systems that have been forgotten, which there are plenty of, further out of date or outright obsoletes them.
Alongside that, part of what made each playthrough special was that you actually had time to take in what you were doing, and every decision you had had at least SOME effect on your empire, as well as the big events were rare, special, and made me want to start a new one over and over, just to see what happened.

Now? Youll click fast for 5 whole seconds, get 15 events, and half of them are just talking about how one of your scientists found another space plankton for 5% shield bonus, or your fourth repairable shipyard. the Marauders will squack at you for .000005% of your total GDP like they are actually a threat, the Khan will wake up and get killed by the closest fallen empire every time without fail.

Then it will go one of two ways: Youll either slog through 100 years of contsant emmisary requests, microing leaders as they die to ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, forgetting to check the galactic council because youre busy doing literally anything else and then a law ♥♥♥♥♥ you, so you leave and commit mass omnicide,

OR

Youll get bored of the same cycle of the game over and over again now by the Khan waking up that youll just stop playing.

TLDR: The way the game feels now makes it play more like the fortnight of 4x games then a story of the rise and fall of space empires, constant jingling keys and all.

SIDE NOTE: the DLC model is also ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ atrocious and now they have made like 60 dollars of DLC I paid for free and I got nothing for it, this shouldnt matter, but it tickles me wrong that I get shafted having paid for every DLC for years.

review:
After 3000 hours into the previous versions of Stellaris and absolutely loving it, taking a long break, now trying it out again. Been recently playing it for around another 200 hours and I can with certainty, the reworks on pops and fleets is absolute unbearable. Pops now make no sense and you have to watch a freaking YouTube video to even understand it and even then it still roller-coasters for no reason. 

And the fleet rework... I lost a 68k fleet to a 38k startbase... do I need to say more.  If we are suppose to rock paper and scissor the Ai builds, then make it easier to do so and not the most expansive espionage operation. And AUTO GATHER INTEL!

As for now, I am done with this game. As someone who has bought all the DLCs and have over 3000 hours into it. It breaks my heart but this game as become garbage now.

Edit: after calming down, I can truely say no. The new fleet rework is just bad, they tried to break the "meta" ship building aspect but in doing so, forces the player to HAVE TO play by research enemy ships, which is just not worth the time and investment, then purposely build to counter it. It is taking away player agency because if you don't do that, you are not winning those fleet engagements. I have legitimate come to feel this game no longer respects my time.

review:
I have been playing this game on and off again since its release and fell in love with its ability to naturaly build a fresh story/narrative during gameplay, changing up each new run, and its strong Customization options.
Sadly over the many years the Content bloat has become quite a lot in a sense that every update new things get added and new things break while the content gets arguably more lazy with each update but not less expensive.

This Wine Barrel of Dissapointment was already filled to the brim and now that they are getting Lazy with their Art and Voices as well by using AI it was the last drop to make it all spill over.

I will forever remember fondly the absolute blast I had with it during the first couple years but it has changed way to much and it's time to move on, and honestly the Devs should move on from this game as well.

Если говорить о сложности то эта игра идеальна она не проходняк где ИИ беспомощный да хойка? Да твою налево хойка где ИИ груша для битья с миллионом капа и ты задушишься его капать. Но одновременно и не какая-то зубодробительная вещь на миллионы часов разбирательств или учтения лютых таймингов (меня щас жестко флешбекнуло на ачивку на создание Австро-Венгрии в хойке до длс Гутердамренг где пришлось буквально дни часы  считать и поведение ИИ заучивать что бы ее сделать)

ИИ здесь реально может дать отпор и если ты ошибешься то ты будешь жалеть ибо если в хойке опять таки же ты можешь за месяц вернуть хоть 3 потеряннх армии то тут ты будешь сидеть и плакать ждать пока флот вернется или молится богам верфей чтобы они не делали 1000 дней какой-нибудь титан

Так же что то или иное делает интуитивно понятно и не убьет (в большинстве случаев) тебя если ты так сделаешь что опять таки же хойка сосет ибо если стелларис я уже разобрался не плохо и без гайдов то в хойке я понял что делать только к 200 часам и то по гайдам!!!

Также реиграбельность куча наций с своими особенностями, куча варитивности внутри этой нации, ХОРОШАЯ ДИПЛОМАТИЯ а не опять таки как в хойке где из дипломатии ты юзаешь только войну, разведка тут рил решает и ею интересно и полезно играть (опять таки снова хойка где шпионами ты ничерта не сделаешь если ИИ не подстроится и если тут пренебрежние шифрованием может привести к восстаниюю всех планет (Столичной тоже вроде) то в хойке ты состаришься пока набьешь нужный уровень идеологии а по итогу 1 хрен восставшие бездарно сольются)

Я могу еще 30 лет приводить сравнения с хойкой ибо я хойщик с 1500 часами (знаю мало но что есть) но я думаю того что написано вполне хватит чтобы понять что в выборе между нудятиной где у 90% стран одинаковая игра и между этим шедевром сделать правильный выбор.

Оптимизация сомнительна но это проблема всех игр студии и уж тут ничего не поделать НО тостер тянет и у тебя потянет сначала ты не почувтсвуешь проблем оптимизации но годам к 3000 поймешь про что я

К игре однозначно рекомендую к покупке ДЛС не рекомендую что вы как дураки(такие деньги на то что и так должно быть в игре учитывая цену 20100 тенге)? Найдите себе анлокер в интернете и кайфуйте (Цена кусается сочувствую тем кто будет покупать за фулл цену ну а меня повезло за 1к тенге урвать до повышения цен) А ну и ждем ДЛС с кочующими нациями (опять таки хойка сосет ибо реализовать такое внутри нее нереально а тут вполне будет интересно выживать)

review:
This is the best terrible videogame I've ever played, or perhaps the worst 7/10 game I've ever enjoyed for cumulatively multiple hundreds of hours. 

Seen as a grand strategy game, Stellaris is a fascinating trainwreck that is slowly being improved at the cost of turning into a hyper-monetized walled garden that would make the likes of Blizzard Entertainment blush. Seen as a generator of emergent narrative, it is perhaps a masterpiece, but one buried beneath the second-clunkiest and most Byzantine user interface I've seen, surpassed in bewilderment only by the inimitable likes of Final Fantasy XIV. It's horrible. It's gorgeous. And the soundtrack absolutely rules. 

I wish I could get those couple hundred hours back, but at the same time, I regret nothing.

review: I have it on gamepass for free.  AI balancing is awful.  Enemies spawning in fleets that they previously didn't have access to or the ability to muster all based on keeping the game going longer.  There are multiple instances I've encountered where the AI should have been in a state beyond repair yet somehow pulls out a large fleet within a month.  It makes for a very boring cat and mouse game.  The upgrade/micromanagement systems are pretty tedious.

ein großes Lob and Paradox das sie dieses Spiel rausgebracht haben und es weiter Updaten.

review:
Incredible game BUT : DLC price go brrr, it's good and they're not like dlc slop, it's good, well made, content-heavy DLCs but there are a lot of them, and because they're so conten heavy the base game seems so small without them, it's totally not the case btw, the base game is still really good, the dlcs are just that good, also my reccomendations for said dlcs 

Machine Age is a must if you like robots, Biogenesis is quite good, do reccomend, Lithoids is seen as bad by a lot of people but the species are just incredibly good looking, I use one of them half the time, Infernals is really good too, I would reccomend Nemesis in general but do not buy Cosmic storms or Distant Stars first, they're not as good at the others, I kinda just bought them for the cube species and the jellyfich amalgam species that you unlock when you have all the dlcs, also talking about species, create or loggin into your paradox account, the behemothkin species that you get from it is like in my top five species ever, and it goes well with Biogenesis for obvious reasons (the Behemoth)

also there is a mod workshop with a lot of good things, would reccomend Gigastructural Engineering if you like huge things (attack moon go brrr) or Ancient Cashe Of Technology (ACOT) if you like small but powerful tech, and both at the same time is kinda ridiculous, also the species mods by Silfae are really good, and there is two Star Trek total conversion mods, two Star Wars ones, and a Mass Effect one so a lot of potential, and the youtubers that play the game are generally really funny and the community is the opposite of toxic and kinda funny but on the good side of funny

review:
This is a very complicated strategy game with a lot of depth. You will lose slowly and painfully over and over as you try to figure things out. There's nothing wrong with that if that's your cup of tea.

What's wrong with the game is that the Devs won't leave it the eff alone. DLC and expansions are great...but not if they rewrite the fundamental mechanics every time they want to introduce something new. I was never great at this game, but I could hold my own after a month or so of experimenting. After 100 hours in game, I can no longer play the game that I bought, and the dozens of changes are so disorienting I can't get any traction no matter what I do.

In short, I can't recommend this game unless it's going to be THE GAME you play for a while. If you're dedicated enough to keep up with the changes and master and re-master the systems, there's a lot to play with in here. Most of it's buried under layers of tedious complexity, but some people like that. I have no idea whether the devs are done mucking around, it's been years and they keep doing things to the game.

review:
Paradox seems to never be sure what to do with this game and its success. They have basically remade the game several times from scratch since release and never settled. This shows a clear lack of vision or belief in the systems they made and also makes you relearn the game mechanics every time a new major patch hits. It is both disrespectful towards their own developer's time as well as the player's.

DLCs are an utter scam as per the Paradox standard. Almost all of them are lacking in content and ways to change how the game is played. Generally they are not even worth buying on sale. Furthermore, Paradox seems to now embrace generating "content" with AI showing even further disrespect towards their customers. Absolute joke.

It is an absolute shame that this game has been mismanaged to such an extent, but that is the Paradox promise. Play some other 4X game if you want something to scratch that itch.

This shows a clear lack of vision or belief in the systems they made and also makes you relearn the game mechanics every time a new major patch hits. It is both disrespectful towards their own developer's time as well as the player's. DLCs are an utter scam as per the Paradox standard. Almost all of them are lacking in content and ways to change how the game is played. Generally they are not even worth buying on sale. Furthermore, Paradox seems to now embrace generating "content" with AI showing even further disrespect towards their customers. Absolute joke. It is an absolute shame that this game has been mismanaged to such an extent, but that is the Paradox promise. Play some other 4X game if you want something to scratch that itch.

review: This Game feel like a cashgrab. every year, there's some '50$' worth of new (what ill affectionately describe as) 'Content', that keeps trying to update a ten year old engine to, at this point, make more money. Its going to keep increasing its price year-by-year, and that bothers me. as a *game* it is a wonderful game, with unmatched customization compared to the competition, (most of which is published by the same studio,) and yet it leaves me longing for something more in-depth. I like the game, but i wouldn't recommend it to anyone, (atleast anyone looking to buy all of it legitimately,) in this economy.

review:
Habe das Spiel vor Jahren recht viel gespielt und wollte es jetzt mal wieder angehen.
Ich war ja so enttäuscht. Ein Spiel starten bot nicht viel Optionen. Zufall war immer ein ziemlich sinnlos zusammengestelltes Imperium.
Wollte ich ein eigenes erstellen fielen mir bei den Traits schon komische Bezeichnungen auf wie ac_ac_immortal und so etwas ähnliches. Also bitte! Das sind vielleicht Progammiernamen, aber so etwas lässt man doch nicht im Spiel!
Die größte Enttäuschung was aber, dass es nicht möglich war, mit einem eigenen Imperium ein Spiel zu starten. Über den Schritt Homeworld kam ich nicht hinaus. Egal, welche Kombination ich vorher ausgewählt hatte, es blieb immer rot. Auch völlig egal welche Welt ich auswählte. 
Auch die ganzen Traits und Origins anzugeigen, wenn man gar keinen Zugriff darauf hat, weil ein DLC fehlt, ist einfach schlechter Stil und Geldhascherei.
Sehr traurig, was aus diesem Spiel geworden ist! 
Völlig überladen und es wirkt, als ob die Entwickler selbst keinen Überblick mehr haben.

review_english: I played the game a lot years ago and wanted to try it again now. I was so disappointed. Starting a game didn't offer many options. Chance was always a rather pointlessly assembled empire. When I wanted to create my own, I noticed strange names in the traits like ac_ac_immortal and something similar. So please! These may be programming names, but you don't leave something like that in the game! The biggest disappointment, however, was that it wasn't possible to start a game with your own empire. I didn't get past the Homeworld step. No matter what combination I had previously selected, it always remained red. It didn't matter which world I chose. Also, using all the traits and origins when you don't have access to them because there is no DLC is simply bad form and a money grab. Very sad what has become of this game! Completely overloaded and it seems as if the developers themselves no longer have an overview.

review:

Every couple of months my group considers playing, one of us boots up the game and looks and we all decide not to play. 

I cannot in good consciousness recommend this game given paradox's current model and the state of the game.

review:
I can say with a full certainty, that to enjoy the game you need at least some of the DLC's. Base game is not enough to truly get into this game. And its NOT worth it.

I adore this game. The roleplay potential, the ability to have fun time in multiplayer with your friends, the stories. But the full experience with all the dlc's would cost me over 250 Euro, or half of it if I bothered with keys. Each of the DLC is grossly overpriced, and it feels like they are constructed in a way that makes them something you need for full experience, rather than something purely optional.

2/10. I am disappointed by an amazing game clearly being used to blatantly milk people.

review:
戦争、内政、外交、どれも薄っぺらく平坦で、現状では面白いゲームではありません。
プレイ時間が常に中だるみしているような印象で、戦闘も主力艦隊の決戦くらいしかすることがなく、奥行きがなく退屈でしかもプレイ時間はやたらと長い。
他のパラドゲーを参考にしてほしかった。虚無に近いSPOREの宇宙モードの方がまだ楽しい。

一例ですが、研究について…
・何を研究できるかはランダム！なので、戦略性が無い
　これを最初に開発して、次にこれを取って…という進め方はできない
　個々人の戦略を考えることはできないぞ！
・項目が猛烈な数あるのに全て5%とか10%のバフで効果が少なすぎカタルシス皆無
　普通のゲームでは一個の項目を小分けにしてバラしたような感じ
　よって操作は忙しいのに効果は見えず常に中だるみする。。

なんか総体としてやたらにプレイ時間を増やすため、水増しに水増しを重ねているような印象。なんでや？？
AOEとかより1ゲームのプレイ時間は30倍かかるが、楽しみは1/3。逆にしてくれ！
少なくとも、マルチでこのゲームは絶対やりたくないぞ！！カタルシスが無いのに操作量は多いし！

review_english: War, Domestic Affairs, and Diplomacy are all thin and flat, so it's not an interesting game at the moment.The play time always feels sluggish.The battles are limited to decisive battles between the main fleets.There's no depth, it's boring, and the play time is extremely long.I wish they'd looked at other parado games.SPORE's near-empty space mode is still more fun, but as an example, what you can research is random, so there's no strategy. You can't proceed by developing this first and then taking this.You can't think of individual strategies.There are a huge number of items, but all the buffs are 5 or 10, so the effect is too small and there is no catharsis.In a normal game, it feels like one item has been broken down into small parts.Therefore, even though the operations are busy, you can't see the effect and it's always sluggish.I get the impression that they are adding more and more in order to increase the play time as a whole. Why, it takes 30 times longer to play one game than AOE or something, but the fun is 1 3 Please turn it around, at least I don't want to play this game in multiplayer mode.There is no catharsis, but there is a lot of control.

review:
I have played Stellaris from the very beggining when tile based planets were a thing. 

Paradox has completly redesigned the gameplay (specially economy/construction) at least 4 times which is a chore re-learning each time.

At the time of this review a DLC from 2016 that is literally a couple ship types (apocalypse) costs 20€.
Paradox will only learn greed doesn't pays when we vote with our wallets.

I love this game and I have sunk a lot of time in it but I won't buy any other DLC from Paradox or continuing supporting them.

Also they collect tons of personal data like IP, browser type and gameplay behaviour.

Paradox has completly redesigned the gameplay (specially economy construction) at least 4 times which is a chore re-learning each time. At the time of this review a DLC from 2016 that is literally a couple ship types (apocalypse) costs 20 . Paradox will only learn greed doesn't pays when we vote with our wallets. I love this game and I have sunk a lot of time in it but I won't buy any other DLC from Paradox or continuing supporting them. Also they collect tons of personal data like IP, browser type and gameplay behaviour.

review:
How you feel about Stellaris depends on your personal view on the DLC, your personal feelings about a game that changes over time, and your personal feelings toward use of generative AI.

If the latter is a dealbreaker, Stellaris is not a game you can play. They began using it around the Machine Age DLC, and do not appear to have walked it back since. They also have not doubled down on using it significantly more, that I have seen. Your own morals must determine where you stand on that line, but it is enough to give it a hard recommend against from me.

But if that's not a dealbreaker for you, consider the lack of consistency. Stellaris changes its face every two years, altering major game systems. When it released there were three forms of FTL travel. That was cut down significantly, with the other methods being added back piecemeal over time. Population was the gate for how well a game could run on weaker processors for half a decade, and at least two major reworks were designed to fix that specific issue. I do not know if it succeeded, in their most recent endeavor. This periodically breaks every mod that is made, and this is a game built on a strong modding community. The good news is that you CAN launch previous versions of the game without great issue; the unfortunate is that you must work much harder to have the right mods for the version of your choice, if it is not the most recent. Some will simply not be available, others requiring greater personal research and non-workshop methods.

If you don't like mods, however, that can be seen as a good thing. It's a game that has had consistent support for over a decade. It has also had consistent DLC. You can see the price easily enough, buying it all won't be cheap. And the rate at which things are released, and the lack of discounts over time, means that unless you're looking at a significant sale right at this moment you may very well get the best deal out of simply... getting the subscription. After all, they DO appear to still be releasing DLC currently, more than once a year. They elongate the timeframe that it is worthwhile even as you use it. And the DLC is very much an all-or-nothing deal; several introduce mechanics that other DLC expand on. Get only one, and you have a half-finished product. And with the updates to the base game, you will feel like you are missing out when returning later, even if you buy up to present and feel content there.

Stellaris is a game I loved. I want to support the people who made it happen. But I don't want to support the higher ups who would replace the people who made it happen with generative AI. So at this time, it's very hard to recommend, even with as much time as I have sunk into the game, and as much as I may yet sink in.

review: I have a soft spot for this game, as I've been playing it on-and-off since close to its initial release. It pains me to feel compelled to make a negative review here, but between the dev's fuzzy acceptance of GenAI use in the office (which is small and 'ethical' right now, but what about later?), and the remarkably small efforts to make the broader game experience affordable for people who are interested in getting into it now, I really don't see a good reason to recommend this game to anyone in its current state. The base game is pretty comparatively bare, and there's a lot of cooler things you can buy with hundreds of dollars than Stellaris DLC.

review:
I have enjoyed this game A LOT in the past. Many long sessions with several people in big fun multiplayer sessions and this is what me and my friends primarily play stellaris for. The fun experience of a 4X/Grand strategy game like stellaris with the human diplomacy interaction. However the focus of paradox just like most of their other games has shifted. It is no longer about making sure that the game is good and stable and works and then release more content. It is 100% "Churn out meh content and charge lots for it" The old paradox is starting to look like they are dead. 

The result of the above is that you CANNOT currently play a multiplayer game even 40% of a "Whole session" as you will start getting desyncs every few seconds to the point that everyone will rage quit as it means you spend 20 seconds to resync everyone every 10-60 seconds (even with just 2-3 players) OR you might be able to do a full rehost of the save which takes 2-5 minutes to do and you MIGHT get another 5-15 minutes out of that before the cycle starts again with every few seconds.

Thanks to that i unfortunately in its current state have to give this a negative rating and it will not change until things change drasticly.

review:
The game itself is fun, and I enjoy it. But unfortunately, it follows an annoying trend in video games: it has a launcher. The funny thing is, the game runs great. But the launcher does not.

Sometimes it crashes, sometimes it freezes, sometimes it hangs. And it doesn't launch the game properly. It's built on Electron and the Chromium version it uses interacts poorly with Wayland.

Since the game doesn't launch, this review is going to have to be negative.

review:
Sumarycznie jest to najlepsze Grand Strategy od Paradoxu - cywilizacje można budować bardzo różnorodnie, i przekłada się to na faktycznie inne doświadczenie w warstwie gameplayu. Systemów jest cała mnogość, ale jednocześnie nie każdy trzeba od razu optymalizować, aby grać sensownie, co sprawia że próg wejścia dla nowych graczy jest niższy od pozostałych GS. Muzyka jest bardzo klimatyczna, a warstwa wizualna pozwala na solidną immersję. Pomniejsze problemy to AI, które nie zawsze radzi sobie w late gamie, oraz postępująca degradacja wydajności wraz z długością trwania kampanii.
Nietypową cechą Stellaris jest to, że twórcy co jakiś czas wprowadzają duże aktualizacje, które nieraz kompletnie przebudowują podstawowe systemy gry. Czasem szkoda pożegnać część Stellaris którą się lubiło, ale zmiany raczej są trafione, a grę można odkrywać na nowo rok po roku, więc wolniej się nudzi.
review_clean: Sumarycznie jest to najlepsze Grand Strategy od Paradoxu - cywilizacje można budować bardzo różnorodnie, i przekłada się to na faktycznie inne doświadczenie w warstwie gameplayu. Systemów jest cała mnogość, ale jednocześnie nie każdy trzeba od razu optymalizować, aby grać sensownie, co sprawia że próg wejścia dla nowych graczy jest niższy od pozostałych GS. Muzyka jest bardzo klimatyczna, a warstwa wizualna pozwala na solidną immersję. Pomniejsze problemy to AI, które nie zawsze radzi sobie w late gamie, oraz postępująca degradacja wydajności wraz z długością trwania kampanii. Czasem szkoda pożegnać część Stellaris którą się lubiło, ale zmiany raczej są trafione, a grę można odkrywać na nowo rok po roku, więc wolniej się nudzi.
review_english: Overall, this is the best Grand Strategy from Paradox - civilizations can be built in many different ways, which translates into a truly different gameplay experience. There are a lot of systems, but at the same time not all of them need to be optimized immediately to play sensibly, which means that the entry threshold for new players is lower than for other GS. The music is very atmospheric, and the visuals allow for solid immersion. Minor problems include AI, which does not always cope with the late game, and the progressive degradation of performance over the duration of the campaign. An unusual feature of Stellaris is that the developers periodically introduce large updates that sometimes completely rebuild the basic game systems. Sometimes it's a pity to say goodbye to a part of Stellaris that you liked, but the changes are rather spot-on, and the game can be rediscovered year after year, so it gets boring slower.

review:
Used to like this game - even modded it - but nowadays, it's genuinely unenjoyable.

Pretty much all the interesting features are locked behind DLC, some of which is genuinely terrible, such as Cosmic Storms, and playing the game increasingly feels like a chore. The setting (or attempt to establish one) is uninteresting, and suffers from a lack of consistency and it not meshing very well with the type of game Stellaris is (For instance, why are there apparently three separate species, namely the pre-FTL Pyorun, the Olinbar of MSI, and the.. Yatunans.. that all share the same portrait, and have zero relation to one another?).

Generally, inconsistency is something very prominent with Stellaris in general Even the portraits aren't consistent in art style - it feels very jarring to see newer portraits next to old ones thanks to the art style somehow changing so much that the perspective went from a bust to showing 3/4ths of the body, meaning that the room backgrounds used in diplomacy do not feel consistently sized at all. Many of the game's portraits also fail to be visually interesting - one particular Humanoid portrait even lacks the variety that was shown in advertising.

Even Paradox itself has better offerings when it comes to strategy games. Stellaris just isn't very good anymore, and given the amount of DLC, I doubt it'll improve.

review: While I have few hours on Steam I have been playing this game since 2020 on console and likely have hundreds if not thousands of hours there. This has got to be my favorite game of all time. Something about creating your own overarching sci fi story that lasts generations is something that most games do not have. Sadly I cannot recommed it due to the amount of predatory dlc and the new update. There was no reason for them to completely overhaul the games mechanics and the way economy works I was very happy with the gameplay loop they had and this new planetary system is just not good. The dlc while adding good content should have been mostly free updates. Please find something cheaper to spend you time on.

review:
Stellaris is still a good game, but it feels like it has drifted away from what originally made it special.

Many newer DLCs focus on highly specific fantasy-style narratives, scripted event chains and special mechanics, while traditional empire archetypes such as slavers, megacorps, hive minds and many older civics and ethics receive comparatively little attention. What once felt like emergent science fiction now often feels like a collection of pre-written adventures layered on top of a grand strategy game.

The development focus seems to have shifted from empire simulation towards narrative content. Regardless of the empire you start with, many games increasingly revolve around pursuing the same major progression systems and ascension paths. The defining feature of an empire often ends up being how it ascended rather than the ethics, civics or government it was founded upon.

An irony is that Paradox created the Custodian Team because players felt older mechanics were being left behind, yet many older civics, ethics and empire archetypes still feel neglected compared to the constant stream of new narrative systems.

Instead of another story-driven DLC, I'd rather see Paradox revisit older civics, ethics and empire archetypes so that a wider range of playstyles feels genuinely distinctive and worthwhile again. Stellaris doesn't need more stories. Unfortunately, the current DLC model appears far better suited to selling new narrative experiences than revisiting and strengthening the foundations that already exist.

review:
I consider myself a role-play gamer.

Pros:

Interesting ideas about what a fresh civilization in the galaxy could encounter, like fallen empires, end-game crises, archaeology, Astral rifts, and all kinds of random events.

It provokes my thoughts about what economics and geopolitics can be in the interstellar era, and how high tech is not equal to high ethics. (I act like a Stalin-era command economy, so I thought it couldn't be in the real world. And you can still be Militarist, Xenophobe, or Imperial even if you can travel at light speed.)

It provides a basic idea of how war can be in the interstellar era. (It's basic because you cannot control like Homeworld when fights start.)

It contains all forms of civilization, like Necroids (if the fungus in The Last of Us had intelligence), Hive Minds (like that in Pluribus), and Machine Intelligence (like the Geth in Mass Effect).

Cons:

The AI empires are not smart; they close their borders for no reason, even if a fallen empire has awakened or an end-game crisis is at their door.

The economy in 4.3 is a disaster. I constantly encounter a resource deficit (after studying the videos), and the Class-3 and 4 Singularities are a joke! I have to mod the game and use the console to avoid getting overwhelmed by Growth Splinters.

Many things break the immersion, and you can only treat it as a numbers game; you cannot take it seriously.

I won't recommend this game, because I can't control my time put into this game. I thought I'd finish a small goal, but it took me hours. It frustrates me to find I still need lots of time to finish this game. Maybe it's because the economy in 4.3 makes me unable to sit and relax, maybe it's because it doesn't have a natural stopping point. I don't like this type of game, it makes me feel I didn't accomplish anything if I quit mid-game.

review:
Раньше я относился с особой любовью к этой игре. Это уникальная задумка, которая в своё время принесла много положительных впечатлений. Однако в последние годы разработчики разочаровывали и разочаровывали. Если я сейчас играю, то только в старые версии несколько-летней давности — спасибо, хоть это оставили. Вот какие самые большие минусы этой игры с моей точки зрения:

1) Грабительская политика разработчиков. Думаю, всем известно, что параходы самые алчные разработчики. Возможно только разработчики стар ситизен могут побороться с параходами за первое место в этой номинации. Выпущено огромное количество длс, каждое из которых стоит как самостоятельная игра. Представляю, что испытывают люди, которые первый раз видят стелларис в магазине стима.
2) Сверхперегруженная игровая механика. О боже, просто миллиарды разных ресурсов, перков, цивиков, того, сего. Я учусь на мехмате и привык к напряжённой мыслительной деятельности, но воспринимать эту игру просто невозможно. Всё это при том, что такое количество разнообразия ничем не оправданно. Ни к чему эти сплавы, они просто не нужны. Не нужны товары массового спроса. Это делает игру не только сложнее для игроков, но и для самих разработчиков. Вы только представьте себе, как такое сбалансировать. Многие ртс, которые были гораздо проще устроены, и те не сбалансированы идеально. Вместо того, чтобы просто починить то, что есть, разработчики пихают и пихают какую-то неоправданную хрень.
Наверное, в каждом патче есть какая-то несбалансированная имба, что убивает весь остальной геймплей либо заставляет хостов писать правила и запрещать разные механики.
Чтобы разобраться, как играть, разработать какую-то стратегию, нужно заходить на стратегиум и буквально читать по порядку патчноуты и узнавать, как что работало. Нужно сидеть и ботать, как в это играть. И не один день.
3) Тупой ИИ. Вообще этим страдают, наверное, все ртс. Если повышать сложность компьютерных противников, то им просто добавляются ресурсы. Мне всегда это было неинтересно. Конечно, это непросто решить с точки зрения разработчика. Но почему модеры пытались, а разработчики стеллариса нет? Я пробовал Starnet AI, когда он работал — играть становилось интереснее. Самые алчные разрабы не могут выделить денег на улучшение ИИ? Вы бы могли заплатить энтузиастам, которые итак бесплатно пытаются чинить ваше разбитое корыто
4) Бесконечная забагованность, в том числе мультиплеера. Возвращаясь к прошлому пункту, вместо того, чтобы добавить ещё 5 видов ресурсов и 200 гражданских моделей, почему не направить силы команды разработки на тестирование и исправление багов? Вы сами то в свою игру играете?

Это всё, что было в голове, при том, что я давно не слежу за игрой и не играю. Без сомнений есть ещё примеры маразма алчных разработчиков. Ужасно.

review_english: I used to have a special love for this game. This is a unique idea, which at one time brought many positive impressions. However, in recent years, developers have been disappointing and disappointing. If I play now, then only the old versions from several years ago, thank you, at least they left that. Here are the biggest disadvantages of this game from my point of view: 1) The predatory policies of the developers. I think everyone knows that steamships are the most greedy developers. Perhaps only Star Citizen developers can compete with the ships for first place in this category. A huge number of DLCs have been released, each of which stands as an independent game. I can imagine what people experience when they see Stellaris for the first time in the Steam store. 2) Overloaded game mechanics. Oh God, just billions of different resources, perks, civics, this, that. I am a mechanics and mathematics student and am used to intense mental activity, but it is simply impossible to perceive this game. All this despite the fact that such an amount of diversity is in no way justified. These alloys are of no use, they are simply not needed. No need for consumer goods. This makes the game not only more difficult for players, but also for the developers themselves. Just imagine how to balance this. Many rts, which were much simpler in design, are not perfectly balanced. Instead of just fixing what is there, the developers keep pushing and shoving some unjustified crap. Probably, in every patch there is some kind of unbalanced imbalance that kills the rest of the gameplay or forces the hosts to write rules and prohibit various mechanics. To figure out how to play, to develop some kind of strategy, you need to go to the strategium and literally read the patch notes in order and find out how things worked. You need to sit and figure out how to play it. And not just one day. 3) Dumb AI. In general, probably all RTS suffer from this. If you increase the complexity of computer opponents, then they simply have more resources. I was always uninterested in this. Of course, this is not easy to solve from a developer's point of view. But why did the modders try, but the Stellaris developers didn’t? I tried Starnet AI, when it worked, the game became more interesting. The most greedy developers cannot allocate money to improve AI? You could pay the enthusiasts who are already trying to fix your broken trough for free 4) Endless bugs, including multiplayer. Returning to the previous point, instead of adding 5 more types of resources and 200 civilian models, why not direct the development team's efforts to testing and fixing bugs? Do you play your own game? This is all that was in my head, despite the fact that I haven’t been following the game or playing for a long time. Without a doubt, there are more examples of the insanity of greedy developers. Terrible.

review: It pains me to write a negative review for this game. While i might not have nearly as many hours as some others i still genuinely enjoyed the game before the 4.3 patch. Sadly with the new patch they decided to nerve everything into the ground. I am sure a lot of it was good for performance and toning things down was somewhat necessary as previous DLC have made it quite easy to break the game within the first couple years. However for me it makes the game feel a lot smaller and slower than before. Almost to the point where having a particular focus on doesnt seem to put me ahead of other empires in that given field. While i cant speak for anyone, for me personally this update added around 50-100 Years of playtime to any given run. However that time is mostly spent just ramping up. It feels as though a run is just padded out because everything takes way longer. review:
So I've had Stellaris for quite a while now, it's really enjoyable even if you have to relearn it every year or so when an update comes out. The multiplayer is amazing provided you find someone else to play it with you (you won't), and all the story lines and side plots are really quite cute. 

Unfortunately the amount of DLC necessary to enjoy it to its full capacity is slightly ludicrous, especially when you haven't been slowly collecting them over the years. But that's not really a surprise for a paradox game.

Overall I'd definitely recommend giving it a go, just keep in mind that you'll probably spend more time thinking about playing this game than actually playing it.

review:
Considering my hours, at the time of writing being about 700hrs, I hope I can say that I have a good understanding of the game and that I enjoy it. However, as much time I have spent playing, and not matter how much I enjoy it, to get all the DLC and content should not be over $200. There shouldn't be a Subscription Service as an option to enjoy all the content, solely because of the outragous prices. Especially for the amazing and fabled [Insert Species] Pack, that only provide different looks, and give no difference to gameplay, at the cost of $15 each!

Granted, there are some DLC's that are genuinely great, though once more not at the prices set. If any game represented the resounding issues present within Gaming. It would be seen clearly through Paradox, and particularly through Stellaris.

review: This game was incredibly fun a few years ago. I came back to the new update. I cannot recommend this game to new players. They really broke how fleets worked. Ships are very expensive but also now very weak; strong fleets regularly get wiped by weaker fleets and the interface doesn't give you any option to analyze why that happened. AI is vastly overpowered even on the lower difficulty settings. Pass on this one until Paradox fixes what they broke.

review:
I have over 40 hours on this game. I like it when it works. Unfortunately, it almost never works. I have yet to actually finish a single game of Stellaris, because each time I have tried to start a game, I run into a game breaking bug that seems to be well-known in the community (or at least documented elsewhere online). 

For instance, I once had a single Empire (given the single same-colored realm of shared territory and shared name?) somehow spawn with two Homeworlds and different two leaders/governments? I had to go through first contact with them twice (given the two Homeworlds/governments), but everything else seemed shared between them, including intel, opinion, etc., ... after a bit of trying to continue the game (especially when they went to war), it just completely broke the galaxy.

review: How to describe what Stellaris means to me. This is a game I cannot get out of my head as it has everything I love about gaming: fantastic stories, great challenges, science fiction, role playing aspects, great visuals and arguably the best game sound track ever made if not the best sound track full stop. The systems as always with grand strategy games are complex and intertwined, but I would argue that the tutorial mechanism of Stellaris is just enough of a nudge to start exploring the rest yourself, and boy does this game keep giving. Endless opportunities to build and roleplay an empire, and no run is the same as this game is an endless story generator with events, personal grudges, manoeuvring, warfare, the list never ends. At the time of writing I am almost 200 hours in and I feel I have barely scratched the surface and I still look forward to more. The game also has beautiful artwork ant writing, making it a joy to learn what is happening in your empire. Much is made of Paradox's DLC business model, but I must admit I really enjoy the new content as it gets released, allowing to play around with new factions, stories and mechanics. An underrated aspect for me is how well the Stellaris team creates the lore and background of their galaxies and expansions. Their expansion trailers are nothing short of astounding and the Stellaris chords at the end of every video are enough to draw me back for another game. Stellaris is my always game that I love playing on my steam deck and on the big screen and it will not be long before it becomes my most played game of all time. Stellaris is in a continuous fight with the Mass Effect Trilogy for the top spot in my top 10, and the best way I can phrase it is that if Mass Effect is my greatest game of all time then Stellaris is my favourite way to spend my gaming time. If you even remotely like sci fi or strategy gaming, this one is a must.

review:
最开始接触这款游戏是在20年的高中时期，用的设备是初代普通m1芯片的macbookpro。当时觉得苹果的东西就是先进，买了到手才发现，大部分的游戏都玩不了（笑）而群星是少数能在mac上原生运行的游戏。我从小就很喜欢太空，喜欢宇宙，在ipad2时代里的浴火银河2给我留下过难以磨灭的印象，因此在看到群星也是太空游戏并且评价不错后，我就果断想试试—然后入了学习版（笑)不能否认的是，群星陪伴了我从高中到大学，这游戏在我看来游戏性反而一般—学习成本高，版本变动大。但独属于他的太空歌剧的浪漫感是任何游戏都无法比拟的，他的交战画面很简陋，很多事件仅仅只有一个插图一段文字，没有华丽的战斗演出，没有精彩的剧情cg，但他能给人留下无限的遐想空间。我记得我曾经无数次对着小屏幕里一卡一卡的交战画面里幻想太空战的演出，挖掘遗迹时幻想遗迹里的风光，管理星球区划建设时幻想每个星球上每个人的生活。我也记得我也对着屏幕里的堕落帝国喊着豆浆拜倒，面对着被地爆天星炸掉的星球说着高效。如今我大学即将毕业，我也有了自己的pc，我可以在东京飙车，可以在洛圣都为所欲为，可以去当一只上天入地的猴子，但我永远忘不掉的是我脑海里的群星。
PS:这游戏dlc怎么这么贵？

review_english: I first came into contact with this game when I was in high school 20 years ago. The device I used was a MacBook Pro with the first generation ordinary m1 chip. At that time, I thought that Apple’s products were advanced. After I bought it, I realized that most of the games could not be played. Lol. Stellaris is one of the few games that can run natively on a Mac. I have loved space and the universe since I was a child. Galaxy on Fire 2 in the iPad 2 era left an indelible impression on me. So after seeing that Stellaris was also a space game and the reviews were good, I decided to try it and joined the learning version. Laughs) What cannot be denied is that Stars accompanied me from high school to college. This game seems to me to be mediocre in terms of gameplay, high learning costs, and big version changes. But the romantic sense of its unique space opera is unmatched by any game. Its battle scenes are very simple. Many events only have an illustration and a paragraph of text. There are no gorgeous battle performances and no wonderful plot cg. But it can leave people with unlimited room for imagination. I remember that I have fantasized about space battle performances in the battle scenes on the small screen countless times. When excavating ruins, I fantasize about the scenery in the ruins. When managing planet zoning and construction, I fantasize about the lives of everyone on each planet. I also remember that I also shouted soy milk and bowed down to the fallen empire on the screen. Facing the planet blown up by the Earth Explosion Star, I talked about efficiency. Now I am about to graduate from college. I also have my own PC. I can race in Tokyo, do whatever I want in Los Santos, and be a monkey in the sky. But what I will never forget is the stars in my mind. PS Why is this game's DLC so expensive?

review:
One of the best games I have played, but no game is perfect, and Stellaris is not perfect.
Some things you should keep in mind before buying:
1. DLC adds a large amount of depth to the game, and that what can be considered "meta" is constantly changing, like how some mechanics from one dlc might be made redundant in the next update/dlc.
2. The AI, even after 10 years is still not good (ex. Fleets attacking starbases first in a system with hostile enemy fleets or occupying a planet in a existential campaign and surveying that system may be broken).
3. AI was used to make a hostile AI voice, an advisor voice, and artwork reference material, but not any outright generative AI artwork in the game.

Still a wonderful 4X game with lots of roleplay options and interesting player crisis paths, the lore goes crazy too.
9/10

review:
星海之间，遇见无限可能——《群星》玩后感

若是半个月前有人告诉我，我会为了一款游戏连续熬夜，甚至在本该用于休息的凌晨三点，还在为某个遥远星系里一颗矿星的赤字而焦头烂额，我定然会嗤之以鼻。 然而此刻，望着电脑屏幕上那片熟悉的银河缓缓旋转，我不得不承认，Paradox的《群星》确实做到了——它在我心中种下了一片星海。

初入《群星》的体验，坦白说并不算愉快。 满屏的UI界面、纷繁复杂的资源种类、令人眼花缭乱的舰船设计，以及那些诸如“科研船”“工程船”“宣称”“厌战度”等闻所未闻的术语，几乎在第一时间就击溃了我的自信 。 我不止一次地在各种论坛上搜索“群星新手入门”，看到许多老玩家调侃这是“P社游戏里最容易上手的”，我甚至会怀疑我们玩的是否是同一款游戏 。 第一次开档，选择了预设的“地球联合国”，怀揣着和平探索的乌托邦之梦，结果却因为完全不懂如何调配资源，导致能源币和矿物双双赤字，科研停滞，最后在一片茫然中被隔壁的人联舰队轻松剿灭。

如果故事停在这里，这大概又是一篇劝退新手的“避雷指南”。 但《群星》的魅力恰恰在于，当你咬牙跨过那道陡峭的学习曲线后，隐藏在重重迷雾背后的，是一个足以让任何科幻爱好者心潮澎湃的宏大宇宙。

这款游戏给予玩家的第一重震撼，是前所未有的自由与代入感。 你不再是跟随既定剧情的主角，而是一个文明真正的缔造者。 从种族特质、政体形态，到伦理倾向、起源故事，每一个选项都在定义“你是谁” 。 是成为军国排外的人类联邦，将异星视为低等生物予以清洗？ 还是扮演爱好和平的亲外唯物主义者，用外交与贸易在银河系广结善缘？ 抑或是抛弃碳基躯壳，飞升为冰冷的机械智能，同化一切有机生命？ 在这里，所有的道德选择都交还给了玩家，而每一次选择，都开启了一段截然不同的故事。 这种深度的角色扮演沉浸感，是其他许多策略游戏难以企及的。

探索的未知与惊喜，构成了游戏前中期的核心乐趣。 每当一艘科研船跃迁至新的星系，那种面对未知的紧张与兴奋便油然而生。 你可能发现一颗完美的殖民星球，也可能唤醒一个沉睡千万年的远古文明； 你或许会遭遇堵住超空间航道的太空 amoeba，也可能在某个不起眼的星球上挖掘出能改写科技进程的异常点 。 这种源源不断的新鲜感，让每一次开局都充满了变数，真正做到了“千星千面”。 正如一位玩家所言，比起《文明》那种相对确定性的发展， 《群星》的宇宙才真正充满了“故事” 。

随着游戏深入，帝国管理的深度与策略性逐渐显现。 从最初的几个星系，到横跨半个银河的庞大帝国，你需要学会如何规划“专精球”——有的星球专职产出能源，有的则成为合金铸造中心，还有的作为纯粹的科研枢纽 。 你要应对内部派系的政治诉求，也要处理错综复杂的外交关系：签订贸易协定、组建联邦、背弃盟约、甚至是在幕后策划一场颠覆性的间谍行动 。 而当战端开启，看着自己精心设计的泰坦舰在舰队战中倾泻火力，那种“我们的舰队遮天蔽日”的成就感，足以让之前所有的经营辛劳都变得值得 。

当然，《群星》并非完美无缺。 后期随着帝国规模扩大，游戏速度会因单核性能瓶颈而显著下降 ； DLC的累计价格也确实不菲，常被玩家戏称为“DLC狂魔”（当然，凝聚力dlc这一块） ； 每次大版本更新更是天翻地覆，让回归的老玩家也常常感觉自己像个新人 。 但换个角度看，这种持续的、近乎颠覆性的更新，也恰恰证明了Paradox对这款游戏的生命力倾注的心血，让玩家“花一个游戏的钱，玩到了好几个游戏” 。

如今，当我在又一个深夜保存游戏，看着那个小小的存档文件，我知道它承载的不仅仅是一串数据，而是属于我的一个文明兴衰史。 从被AI暴打的萌新，到如今能在银河系中游刃有余地斡旋， 《群星》给予我的，远不止是几百小时的娱乐时光。 它让我在星海之间，遇见了关于策略、关于想象、关于抉择的无限可能。

如果你也渴望在浩瀚星河中书写属于自己的史诗，不妨给《群星》一个机会。 只要熬过最初的迷茫，那片璀璨星海，必将回馈你最波澜壮阔的文明传奇。
review_clean: 星海之间 遇见无限可能 群星 玩后感 若是半个月前有人告诉我 我会为了一款游戏连续熬夜 甚至在本该用于休息的凌晨三点 还在为某个遥远星系里一颗矿星的赤字而焦头烂额 我定然会嗤之以鼻 然而此刻 望着电脑屏幕上那片熟悉的银河缓缓旋转 我不得不承认 Paradox的 群星 确实做到了 它在我心中种下了一片星海 初入 群星 的体验 坦白说并不算愉快 满屏的UI界面 纷繁复杂的资源种类 令人眼花缭乱的舰船设计 以及那些诸如 科研船 工程船 宣称 厌战度 等闻所未闻的术语 几乎在第一时间就击溃了我的自信 我不止一次地在各种论坛上搜索 群星新手入门 看到许多老玩家调侃这是 P社游戏里最容易上手的 我甚至会怀疑我们玩的是否是同一款游戏 第一次开档 选择了预设的 地球联合国 怀揣着和平探索的乌托邦之梦 结果却因为完全不懂如何调配资源 导致能源币和矿物双双赤字 科研停滞 最后在一片茫然中被隔壁的人联舰队轻松剿灭 如果故事停在这里 这大概又是一篇劝退新手的 避雷指南 但 群星 的魅力恰恰在于 当你咬牙跨过那道陡峭的学习曲线后 隐藏在重重迷雾背后的 是一个足以让任何科幻爱好者心潮澎湃的宏大宇宙 这款游戏给予玩家的第一重震撼 是前所未有的自由与代入感 你不再是跟随既定剧情的主角 而是一个文明真正的缔造者 从种族特质 政体形态 到伦理倾向 起源故事 每一个选项都在定义 你是谁 是成为军国排外的人类联邦 将异星视为低等生物予以清洗 还是扮演爱好和平的亲外唯物主义者 用外交与贸易在银河系广结善缘 抑或是抛弃碳基躯壳 飞升为冰冷的机械智能 同化一切有机生命 在这里 所有的道德选择都交还给了玩家 而每一次选择 都开启了一段截然不同的故事 这种深度的角色扮演沉浸感 是其他许多策略游戏难以企及的 探索的未知与惊喜 构成了游戏前中期的核心乐趣 每当一艘科研船跃迁至新的星系 那种面对未知的紧张与兴奋便油然而生 你可能发现一颗完美的殖民星球 也可能唤醒一个沉睡千万年的远古文明 你或许会遭遇堵住超空间航道的太空 amoeba 也可能在某个不起眼的星球上挖掘出能改写科技进程的异常点 这种源源不断的新鲜感 让每一次开局都充满了变数 真正做到了 千星千面 正如一位玩家所言 比起 文明 那种相对确定性的发展 群星 的宇宙才真正充满了 故事 随着游戏深入 帝国管理的深度与策略性逐渐显现 从最初的几个星系 到横跨半个银河的庞大帝国 你需要学会如何规划 专精球 有的星球专职产出能源 有的则成为合金铸造中心 还有的作为纯粹的科研枢纽 你要应对内部派系的政治诉求 也要处理错综复杂的外交关系 签订贸易协定 组建联邦 背弃盟约 甚至是在幕后策划一场颠覆性的间谍行动 而当战端开启 看着自己精心设计的泰坦舰在舰队战中倾泻火力 那种 我们的舰队遮天蔽日 的成就感 足以让之前所有的经营辛劳都变得值得 当然 群星 并非完美无缺 后期随着帝国规模扩大 游戏速度会因单核性能瓶颈而显著下降 DLC的累计价格也确实不菲 常被玩家戏称为 DLC狂魔 当然 凝聚力dlc这一块 每次大版本更新更是天翻地覆 让回归的老玩家也常常感觉自己像个新人 但换个角度看 这种持续的 近乎颠覆性的更新 也恰恰证明了Paradox对这款游戏的生命力倾注的心血 让玩家 花一个游戏的钱 玩到了好几个游戏 如今 当我在又一个深夜保存游戏 看着那个小小的存档文件 我知道它承载的不仅仅是一串数据 而是属于我的一个文明兴衰史 从被AI暴打的萌新 到如今能在银河系中游刃有余地斡旋 群星 给予我的 远不止是几百小时的娱乐时光 它让我在星海之间 遇见了关于策略 关于想象 关于抉择的无限可能 如果你也渴望在浩瀚星河中书写属于自己的史诗 不妨给 群星 一个机会 只要熬过最初的迷茫 那片璀璨星海 必将回馈你最波澜壮阔的文明传奇
review_english: Between the Sea of Stars, Encountering Infinite Possibilities, Stars, After-Playing Thoughts If someone had told me half a month ago that I would stay up late for a game, and even worry about the deficit of a mineral star in a distant galaxy at three o'clock in the morning when I should be resting, I would have scoffed. However, at this moment, looking at the familiar Milky Way slowly rotating on the computer screen, I have to admit that Paradox's Stars has indeed done it. It has planted a sea of stars in my heart. The experience of entering Stars for the first time. Frankly speaking, it was not a pleasant experience. The screen-full UI interface, the complicated resource types, the dazzling ship design, and the unheard terms such as scientific research ship, engineering ship, declaration, war exhaustion, etc. almost immediately destroyed my self-confidence. I searched for "Stars Beginner's Guide" on various forums more than once. I saw many old players ridiculing that this is the easiest to get started in P Club games. I even doubted whether we were playing the same game. First time opening the game. I chose the default Earth United Nations with a utopian dream of peaceful exploration. However, because I didn’t understand how to allocate resources, it led to a deficit in both energy coins and minerals, and scientific research stagnated. In the end, I was easily wiped out by the Human Fleet next door in a daze. If the story stopped here, this would probably be another lightning protection guide to discourage novices. But the charm of Stars lies precisely in what is hidden behind the fog after you grit your teeth and cross the steep learning curve. It is a majestic universe that is enough to make any science fiction lover's heart beat. The first shock this game gives players is an unprecedented sense of freedom and agency. You are no longer the protagonist following the established plot, but the true founder of a civilization. From racial traits, political forms to ethical tendencies, origin stories, every option defines who you are. Whether to become a militaristic and exclusive human federation and cleanse alien planets as inferior creatures, or to play the role of a peace-loving xenophilic materialist. Use diplomacy and trade to build good relationships in the galaxy. Or abandon the carbon-based body, ascend to a cold mechanical intelligence, and assimilate all organic life. Here, all moral choices are handed back to the player, and each choice opens a completely different story. This deep role-playing immersion is unmatched by many other strategy games. The unknown and surprises of exploration constitute the core fun of the early and middle stages of the game. Whenever a scientific research ship jumps to a new galaxy, the tension and excitement of facing the unknown will arise spontaneously. You may find a perfect colony planet. You may also wake up an ancient civilization that has been dormant for tens of millions of years. You may encounter space amoeba that blocks the hyperspace channel, or you may discover an anomaly on an inconspicuous planet that can rewrite the technological process. This constant sense of freshness makes every start full of variables. It is truly a thousand stars and a thousand faces. As one player said, compared to the relatively deterministic development of civilization, the universe of stars is truly full of stories. As the game progresses, the depth and strategy of empire management gradually become apparent. From the first few galaxies to a huge empire spanning half the galaxy, you need to learn how to plan and specialize. Some planets are dedicated to producing energy, some become alloy casting centers, and some are purely scientific research hubs. You have to deal with the political demands of internal factions, and deal with complex diplomatic relations. Sign trade agreements, form federations, betray alliances, and even plan a subversive espionage operation behind the scenes. And when the war begins, watch your carefully designed Titan ships pour out firepower in fleet battles, the kind that our fleets cover the sky. Of course, Stellaris is not perfect. As the empire expands in the later stage, the game speed will drop significantly due to single-core performance bottlenecks. The cumulative price of DLC is indeed expensive. It is often nicknamed the DLC maniac by players. Of course, the Cohesion DLC is earth-shaking with each major version update, making returning veteran players often feel like newcomers. But from another perspective, this continuous, almost subversive update It just proves Paradox's dedication to the vitality of this game, allowing players to spend the price of one game and play several games. Now, when I saved the game late at night and looked at the small save file, I knew that it carried more than just a bunch of data, but a history of the rise and fall of civilization that belonged to me. From being a newbie who was violently beaten by AI to now being able to navigate the galaxy with ease, the stars gave me far more than hundreds of hours of entertainment. It allowed me to be among the stars. About strategy, about imagination, about the infinite possibilities of choices. If you also long to write your own epic in the vast galaxy, you might as well give the stars a chance. As long as you survive the initial confusion, the bright sea of stars will surely give you the most magnificent legend of civilization.

Multiplayer depends heavily on how the game feels as sometimes I can play for a few hours no desync and other times we'd desync every minute. If Steam allowed a maybe for recommend I'd do that as some DLCs are great and required while others suck, also please stabilize the updates before pushing them.

review:
Космос идеально подходит для того, чтобы переключить фокус с земных забот на масштабные, вечные вещи. Бездонная черная пустота, миллиарды звезд и неведомые миры веками заставляли людей смотреть вверх и забывать о повседневной суете.В астрономии и философии есть потрясающее понятие, которое очень приземляет и одновременно вдохновляет. Оно называется "Эффект обзора".

Находясь в черной пустоте, астронавты видят нашу планету как крошечный, хрупкий, светящийся голубой шарик, защищенный лишь тонкой полоской атмосферы. В этот момент у человека полностью стираются границы в голове. Национальные конфликты, мелкие обиды, личные драмы и бытовые проблемы мгновенно кажутся ничтожными. Приходит глубокое осознание: мы все - пассажиры одного маленького корабля, летящего сквозь бесконечность, и всё, что у нас есть- это мы сами и люди рядом.

Игра в Stellaris как раз дает прикоснуться к этому масштабу. Когда ты управляешь целыми галактиками, строишь торговые союзы и ведешь цивилизации через века, реальное время начинает лететь быстрее, а личные рубежи и ожидания переносятся гораздо легче.

Бездонная черная пустота, миллиарды звезд и неведомые миры веками заставляли людей смотреть вверх и забывать о повседневной суете.В астрономии и философии есть потрясающее понятие, которое очень приземляет и одновременно вдохновляет. Оно называется "Эффект обзора". Находясь в черной пустоте, астронавты видят нашу планету как крошечный, хрупкий, светящийся голубой шарик, защищенный лишь тонкой полоской атмосферы. В этот момент у человека полностью стираются границы в голове. Национальные конфликты, мелкие обиды, личные драмы и бытовые проблемы мгновенно кажутся ничтожными. Приходит глубокое осознание мы все - пассажиры одного маленького корабля, летящего сквозь бесконечность, и всё, что у нас есть- это мы сами и люди рядом. Игра в Stellaris как раз дает прикоснуться к этому масштабу. Когда ты управляешь целыми галактиками, строишь торговые союзы и ведешь цивилизации через века, реальное время начинает лететь быстрее, а личные рубежи и ожидания переносятся гораздо легче.
review_english: Space is ideal for switching focus from earthly concerns to large-scale, eternal things. The bottomless black void, billions of stars and unknown worlds have made people look up and forget about the everyday hustle and bustle for centuries. There is an amazing concept in astronomy and philosophy that is very grounding and inspiring at the same time. It's called the Overview Effect. From the black void, astronauts see our planet as a tiny, fragile, glowing blue ball, protected only by a thin strip of atmosphere. At this moment, the boundaries in a person’s head are completely erased. National conflicts, minor grievances, personal dramas and everyday problems instantly seem insignificant. There comes a deep realization that we are all passengers of one small ship flying through infinity, and all we have is ourselves and the people nearby. Playing Stellaris just gives you a taste of this scale. When you rule entire galaxies, build trade alliances and lead civilizations through centuries, real time begins to fly faster, and personal milestones and expectations are much easier to bear.

review: I've played a lot of Stellaris.  It has, at times, been an addictive experience.  I once played this game for roughly 26 hours straight, albeit with breaks for food and whatnot.  So, why am I not recommending the game?  First off, it has way too much DLC.  Even with sales, it just costs too much to be worthwhile, at least for me.  Secondly, the game keeps changing.  I know it's possible to revert to a previous version of the game but frankly I can't be bothered at this point to learn how to do that.  When seeing a game update makes you lose interest in it for months or even years, something is really wrong.  As for me, it has reached the point where I doubt I'll ever have any interest in this game again.  That said, I did mostly enjoy my time with the game.

review:
游戏短评｜群星（Stellaris）
评分：7.8/10  
标签：太空4X、P社大战略、科幻帝国、叙事生成器

P社太空战略扛鼎之作，把“探索-扩张-开发-征服”的4X玩法玩出了史诗感。从自定义外星文明起步，坐着科研船摸遍未知星系，拆“宇宙盲盒”的快感直接拉满——有时是满星球金矿的狂喜，有时是撞上利维坦巨兽的惊吓，随机事件总能给你意想不到的科幻故事。

帝国管理深度拉满：科技树、思潮政体、外交博弈、舰队设计，每个系统都有讲究，玩起来像在指挥一整个文明的命运。后期还有天灾入侵、星际联盟等大事件，让宇宙格局瞬间紧张。
美中不足是新手门槛高，前期容易被一堆数值和系统绕晕；中期偶尔会陷入“重复建设”的倦怠期；DLC虽丰富但价格不菲，想玩到完整体验得花不少钱。

如果你爱科幻、能沉下心研究复杂系统，这游戏能让你通宵爆肝——毕竟看着自己的帝国从一颗星球扩张到横跨银河，那种成就感真的没话说。

我感觉自己像个人机。

review_english: Game Short Review Stars Stellaris Rating 7.8 10 Tags Space 4X P Society Grand Strategy Science Fiction Empire Narrative Generator P Company's space strategy is the leading work. The 4X gameplay of exploration-expansion-development-conquest has an epic feel. Starting from a customized alien civilization, traveling around unknown galaxies on a scientific research ship, and the pleasure of dismantling the blind box of the universe is straight away. Sometimes it is the ecstasy of gold mines all over the planet, sometimes it is the shock of bumping into the Leviathan beast. Random events can always give you unexpected science fiction stories. The empire management is full of depth, technology tree, ideological and political system, diplomatic game, fleet design. Each system has its own particularity. It feels like commanding the fate of an entire civilization. In the later stage, there will be natural disaster invasions, interstellar alliances and other major events, which will make the universe tense instantly. If you love science fiction and can concentrate on studying complex systems, this game will keep you up all night. After all, watching your empire expand from one planet to across the galaxy, the sense of accomplishment is truly incredible. I feel like a personal computer.

review:
The true power of this game is in its versatility. You don't just play a space empire in a standard 4x strategy game (although you can, if that's all you're interested in). You get to tell the story of your empire's culture, principles and values, through the countless number of mechanisms introduced in the various expansions. I'll give just one example for clarity:

In one of my playthroughs I was running a Fanatic Materialist empire with equal rights to all sentient beings including AI. I conquered a Fallen Empire where one dominant race held another race in servitude, after genetically altering them to be docile and unintelligent. I reversed the genetic modification and uplifted the slaves to fully functional creatures with full imperial citizenship. As for the "master race", I put them in Residence status with migration and colonization restrictions  for 100 years. As soon as I got a leader candidate from the slave race, I put them as planetary governor over their homeworld. It felt extremely satisfying. 

That's just my "mild" take. A friend of mine played as a fanatic xenophobe who enslaved members of other species and consumed them for food. So there's that too. If you have a good imagination and like RP, Stellaris is your pick.

review: So, deep background, I have not really played video games at all in more than 20 years. I get too focused on them, and that distracts me from other things I need or want to do. But I retired recently, and thought I would give some strategy games a try. This has been my favorite so far. It's a little hard to learn, because there are a lot of subsystems and details. I have not yet been successful at it (half of my ambulatory mushroom empire revolted), but I love it. It is exactly the kind of game I dreamed of as a teenager in the 1970s. Lets you manage the governing of an interstellar civilization and provides pretty much every detail you might think of (I wanted to be able to try to hack the rogue mining drones, which is absolutely something Kirk would do, but there was no option for that). About a decade ago a friend of mine tried to run a play be email game that was basically some early version of Stellaris (Stellar Conquest). It was great, but this is better, not the least because you do not have to maintain a giant spreadsheet for each turn in the game.

review_english: I am a person who can organize even in the most complex crises of the galaxy, adapt to all kinds of environments and challenges, and conquer star systems with his mind, will and discipline. YOU ARE A HUMAN BEING To protect our earthly children from the disgusting evil and minds of evil, dirty slugs. review: Stellaris is a great game and it has gotten better and better over time. I am not too happy with the last updates on fleet management though, as building up fleet to a proper strength to be able to challenge leviathans, strong enemies, and other threats has moved from early mid-game to late mid-game. Nerfing fleet power has turned the game into a building game, as it has become easier to build fortifications than to wage war against someone.

With plenty, plenty, and even more plentiful amounts of amazing mods to keep you entertained, add more fun content, and some other stuff that you might or might not enjoy. Also keep in mind that this game can be impacted by low frames due the amount of stuff it has to process (This has only happened to me on the biggest galaxy map, and i don't know if it will still happen if i go down to a smaller one). Either way, hope you'll enjoy this game as much i as do. Or don't.. I am not the boss of you. (ALSO FAIR WARNING! MOST OF THE CONTENT IS LOCKED BEHIND DLC BECAUSE IS A PARADOX GAME!!! Personally i think it's well worth it, but i can't possibly justify it for someone i don't know.. You have been warned!)

review:
异常事件：漂流瓶
我们的科研船 [麦哲伦号] 在 [阿尔法半人马座星系] 扫描一颗漂流已久的人造物体时，传感器捕捉到了令人难以置信的信号。经过仔细识别，我们万分惊喜地发现，那是旅行者二号。
稚嫩的人类满怀期望地向星海投出了一个漂流瓶，里面装满了他们用所爱的一切搭建出的最珍贵、最友好的礼物。他们想象着这份礼物可能会被外星文明收到，可能会被永久地桎梏在某颗星球的轨道上，可能会被时间撕扯成无意义的碎片——但他们一定不会想到，这份献给未来的礼物的签收人，会是他自己。

review_english: Abnormal event Drift bottle Our scientific research ship Magellan was scanning an artificial object that had drifted for a long time in the Alpha Centauri galaxy. The sensor captured an incredible signal. After careful identification, we were extremely surprised to find that it was Voyager 2. The immature humans threw a drift bottle into the sea of ​​stars with great expectations. It was filled with the most precious and friendly gifts they had built with everything they loved. They imagined that this gift might be received by an alien civilization. They may be permanently shackled in the orbit of a certain planet, and they may be torn into meaningless pieces by time, but they will never think that the signer of this gift to the future will be themselves.

review:
I have an addiction to this game… 4/10.

Every save feels different, and I’ve never really had a playthrough with the same world or race setup twice. Even though I’ve run into a lot of bugs over and over again, I still keep making new playthroughs trying to get the perfect run.

If you want a game that scratches that space itch, you found it. Playing this game honestly makes me want to sit down and write out ways to improve it—that’s how invested I am in it.

So why did I give it a 4 out of 10? Simple. I have standards.

There’s a lot of content, but Stellaris is still heavily focused on colonization. I mean, yeah, it’s in space, but it’s still colonization gameplay with a different skin, and plenty of other games do that too. If the game wants a bigger number, it should switch things up more often. It has more variety than a lot of similar games, which is probably why I’m still addicted to it.

Would I recommend it? Yeah, but not while you have your wallet in your hand. I’m glad they added the subscription option, but making the content cheaper would make more sense.

Until they expand beyond the “aspect of colonizing and competing” mindset, I can’t really rate it higher than this. 4/10.

review_english: It feels like Paradox doesn’t care about the player’s experience of playing Stellaris. This game originally added a lot of attributes and values, plus dozens of planets and fleets in the later stages of the game. Not to mention the computer lags in the later stages of the game, and the complicated operations alone will put you in jail. A truly good game should allow players to get started quickly and allow players to slowly explore more advanced gameplay instead of mindlessly adding Shishan code to the game. What’s even more difficult is that Stellaris has drastically changed the gameplay in several updates. It takes a long time to relearn how to play every major change, and to adjust the balance of game values. If you dare to move the decimal point, do you know what you want to design? The game itself is a good game. I like the alien races and game background design of Stellaris very much. It’s a pity that the attributes and values are really a mess. Can the game planner give a thumbs up to the art team?

review:
Stellaris is one of those games that is 'good' because it has no direct competition. In all fairness, there are a lot of issues with the game, mostly related to the devs. While I have closer to 700 hours on the game(started on xbox, I know),I will say I do enjoy this game and it has a lot to offer. I by no means am saying it is bad. But I AM saying there are things that needed to be fixed a long time ago that simply aren't and the constant game refreshes(changing core mechanics) happens way too often and messes up a lot of us.

I would say stellaris is a 8/10 game because it has no direct competition, but if it did have good comp, it would be more of a 6/10. So still the higher end of average. Stuff like the Tech tree, bad/boring combat mechanics, awkward economic systems, having the game refresh on the core mechanics, and WAY too much DLC all prevent it from being higher.

SO I WILL RECOMMEND STELLARIS AS IT IS A GOOD GAME, but I will say it is def a "mixed, leaning good" type of situation. (Their 10th anniversary was a slap in the face btw...)
