review:
Stellaris is a game I always love coming back to. It is intimidating, it does have a learning curve, and that learning curve is ever shifting as old systems get redesigned. There's nothing quite like it though, the game really lets you live out your space power fantasies. 

Want a space dragon that eats planets? sure go ahead. 
Want to play as rocks that eat rocks and use rocks as currency? nothing wrong with that, it would really rock.
Want to be space Dracula spreading vampirism across the galaxy? its fun.

It's one of the most content rich grand strategy games on the market. Just give it a try, worst case you decide you want to buy all the DLC.

review:
Игра за цивилизацию псиоников:

Дорогая вы газета, во субботу, до рассвета,
Вся Канатчиков планета к телекрану прорвалась,
Вместо чтоб поесть, помыться, уколоться и забыться,
Вся безумная грибница у экрана собралась.

Говорил, ломая руки, Уластар и парагон
Про бессилие науки перед тайнами Покров,
Все мозги разбил на части, все извилины заплел,
И канатчиковы власти колют нам второй укол.

Уважаемый редактор, может лучше про реактор,
Про любимый лунный трактор, ведь нельзя же, год подряд
То тарелками пугают, дескать, подлые, летают,
То у вас амёбы лают, то руины говорят.

Мы кое в чем поднаторели, мы тарелки бьем весь год,
Мы на них уже тиянку съели, если повар нам не врет,
А медикаментов груды - мы в унитаз, кто не дурак,
Вот это жизнь - и вдруг Покровы, вот те раз, нельзя же так.

Мы не сделали скандала, нам вождя недоставало,
Настоящих буйных мало, вот и нету вожаков,
Но на происки и вредни сети есть у нас и бредни,
И не испортят нам обедни злые происки врагов.

Это их худые черти мутят космос во пруду,
Это все придумал Кетлинг, с Ханом ехавший в орду,
Мы про взрывы, про пожары, сочиняли про Червя,
Но тут примчался санитар и зафиксировал меня.

Тех, кто был особо боек, прикрутили к спинкам коек,
Бился в пене параноик, как ведьмак на шабаше:
"Развяжите полотенце, иноверы, изуверцы,
Нам покроверно на сердце и покровно на душе!"

Сорок душ посменно воют, раскалились добела,
Во как сильно беспокоят псионически дела,
Все почти с ума свихнулись, даже кто безумен был,
И тогда главврач Гантуат телекран нам запретил.

Вот он, глаз, в окне маячит, за спиною штепсель прячет,
Подал знак кому-то, значит: фельдшер, вырви провода.
И что ж, нам осталось уколоться, и упасть на дно колодца,
И там пропасть на дне колодца, как в Покровах навсегда.

Ну а завтра спросят дети, навещая нас с утра:
"Папы, что сказали эти психонавты-доктора?"
Мы откроем нашим чадам правду, им не все равно,
Мы скажем: "Удивительное рядом, но оно запрещено!"

Вон псионик, чертов Ов'рон, у него приемник "Зродрон",
Он его ночами крутит, ловит контру зро.
Он там был купцом по шмуткам и подвинулся рассудком,
Ну а к нам попал в волненьи жутком,
С расстревоженным желудком,
Тоже голову свело.

Он прибежал взволнован крайне и сообщеньем нас потряс
Будто наш научный лайнер в Покрове на том погряз,
Сгинул, топливо истратив, весь распался на куски,
И двух безумных наших братьев подобрали рыбаки.

Те, кто выжил в катаклизме, пребывают в пессимизме,
Их вчера в древнейшей призме к нам в псионку привезли,
И один, аквойд-механик, рассказал, сбежав от нянек,
Что Квадратурный многогранник - незакрытый пуп Земли.

"Что там было, как ты спасся?" - каждый лез и приставал,
Но механик тот только трясся и из бластера стрелял,
Он то плакал, то смеялся, то визжал, как кутхолойд,
Он, гад, над нами издевался, ну сумасшедший, тот аквойд?

Взвился бывший гранд-псионик, чернокнижник и крамольник,
Говорит, надо броситься в Покровник, на троих его даешь,
Разошелся, так и сыпет, что Покров как будто выбит,
Будь он параллелепипед, будь он круг, едрена вошь!

Больно бьют по нашим душам голоса за тыщи миль,
Мы зря Гаргантюа не глушим, ой, зря не давим L-врата,
Всей своей враждебной сутью подрывают и вредят,
Кормят, сыпят в нас сто зро про таинственный квадрат.

Лектора из передачи, те, кто так или иначе
Говорят про неудачи и нервируют народ,
Нас берите, обреченных, злобный элдрич вас, ученых,

review_clean: Игра за цивилизацию псиоников Дорогая вы газета, во субботу, до рассвета, Вся Канатчиков планета к телекрану прорвалась, Вместо чтоб поесть, помыться, уколоться и забыться, Вся безумная грибница у экрана собралась. Говорил, ломая руки, Уластар и парагон Про бессилие науки перед тайнами Покров, Все мозги разбил на части, все извилины заплел, И канатчиковы власти колют нам второй укол. Уважаемый редактор, может лучше про реактор, Про любимый лунный трактор, ведь нельзя же, год подряд То тарелками пугают, дескать, подлые, летают, То у вас амёбы лают, то руины говорят. Мы кое в чем поднаторели, мы тарелки бьем весь год, Мы на них уже тиянку съели, если повар нам не врет, А медикаментов груды - мы в унитаз, кто не дурак, Вот это жизнь - и вдруг Покровы, вот те раз, нельзя же так. Мы не сделали скандала, нам вождя недоставало, Настоящих буйных мало, вот и нету вожаков, Но на происки и вредни сети есть у нас и бредни, И не испортят нам обедни злые происки врагов. Это их худые черти мутят космос во пруду, Это все придумал Кетлинг, с Ханом ехавший в орду, Мы про взрывы, про пожары, сочиняли про Червя, Но тут примчался санитар и зафиксировал меня. Тех, кто был особо боек, прикрутили к спинкам коек, Бился в пене параноик, как ведьмак на шабаше "Развяжите полотенце, иноверы, изуверцы, Нам покроверно на сердце и покровно на душе!" Сорок душ посменно воют, раскалились добела, Во как сильно беспокоят псионически дела, Все почти с ума свихнулись, даже кто безумен был, И тогда главврач Гантуат телекран нам запретил. Вот он, глаз, в окне маячит, за спиною штепсель прячет, Подал знак кому-то, значит фельдшер, вырви провода. И что ж, нам осталось уколоться, и упасть на дно колодца, И там пропасть на дне колодца, как в Покровах навсегда. Ну а завтра спросят дети, навещая нас с утра "Папы, что сказали эти психонавты-доктора?" Мы откроем нашим чадам правду, им не все равно, Мы скажем "Удивительное рядом, но оно запрещено!" Вон псионик, чертов Ов'рон, у него приемник "Зродрон", Он его ночами крутит, ловит контру зро. Он там был купцом по шмуткам и подвинулся рассудком, Ну а к нам попал в волненьи жутком, С расстревоженным желудком, Тоже голову свело. Он прибежал взволнован крайне и сообщеньем нас потряс Будто наш научный лайнер в Покрове на том погряз, Сгинул, топливо истратив, весь распался на куски, И двух безумных наших братьев подобрали рыбаки. Те, кто выжил в катаклизме, пребывают в пессимизме, Их вчера в древнейшей призме к нам в псионку привезли, И один, аквойд-механик, рассказал, сбежав от нянек, Что Квадратурный многогранник - незакрытый пуп Земли. "Что там было, как ты спасся?" - каждый лез и приставал, Но механик тот только трясся и из бластера стрелял, Он то плакал, то смеялся, то визжал, как кутхолойд, Он, гад, над нами издевался, ну сумасшедший, тот аквойд? Взвился бывший гранд-псионик, чернокнижник и крамольник, Говорит, надо броситься в Покровник, на троих его даешь, Разошелся, так и сыпет, что Покров как будто выбит, Будь он параллелепипед, будь он круг, едрена вошь! Больно бьют по нашим душам голоса за тыщи миль, Мы зря Гаргантюа не глушим, ой, зря не давим L-врата, Всей своей враждебной сутью подрывают и вредят, Кормят, сыпят в нас сто зро про таинственный квадрат. Лектора из передачи, те, кто так или иначе Говорят про неудачи и нервируют народ, Нас берите, обреченных, злобный элдрич вас, ученых, Превратит в умалишенных, ну а нас - наоборот. Пусть безумная идея, не рубайте сгоряча, Вызывайте нас скорее через гада страж-врача, С уваженьем, дата, подпись, отвечайте нам, а то Если вы не отзоветесь, мы вам всыпем еще зро!

review: It's fun with a variety of gameplay that can span days rather than mere hours. Some folks might not enjoy it because it isn't instantly gratifying, but I like it because I'm nurturing a stellar nation or a cosmic organism that can stretch itself beyond the stars. There are so many origins that offer characterizations that can be modified with countless civics and traits in a sandbox galaxy that never appears to remain static. Fun mechanics or... storylines or the events get me sometimes. Like, I know that the storylines are intentionally interesting despite when I might be playing a devouring swarm, but I always tear up whenever I discover a dead nation that was originally a very happy, sensitive, sentimental and cute civilization that may have crumpled into depression or terror when they discovered the Gray Tempest will one day appear. It's like kneeling down to ask a a little child why they are crying, then learning the reason is because they can't be friends with the school bully.

review:
I played hours and hours of this game on a pirated version, and I finally gave in to the temptation of a sale and bought the Ultimate Bundle.

My wallet will probably regret it in a few months, but my heart won’t. I LOVE this game so much, and I’ve definitely already spent over 500 hours playing it outside of Steam.

Recently, I played multiplayer with some friends through Steam, and it felt so refreshing to experience the new planet management system and mess around with new origins.

Buy it if you can. Just prepare your wallet, because it is way too expensive.

review: I bought this game about 8 years ago and bounced off it. I've only just recently got back into Stellaris and started back up at 4.3, watched some YT streams, did a bit of DLC research, and carefully selected those that were recommended, held off others and own about 2/3 of the whole game now decided the renting gig isn't worth it. Its expensive if you add the costs up, but the game is well improved over the base so I'm finding its pulled me in. I struggle to log out at night and that's exactly what I was hoping for. I've lost interest in a lot of games, so having that immersion again with Stellaris is great and I hope it keeps my interest. So far so good.

无需解释，唯一真神
很高兴在2026年能玩到这么好玩的游戏（尽管这个游戏已经发行了十年），和室友熬夜玩了好多次。打折的时候一定要入，DLC同理，这个游戏DLC越多越好玩。刚开始玩的时候可能会有一点难，但只要玩进去了就没啥问题了，越玩越上头，完全是强化版的文明6（我个人感觉，这两个游戏没有高低之分）。刚玩时候给我的震撼感不亚于小时候玩孢子第一次进入太空的瞬间，真的很少能玩到这样一款如此震撼的游戏了。

However this game is original and is a must have for anyone who wants to get into grand strategy. I played this before trying HoI4 and it gave me a good idea on how to play.
There's a lot of cool events and easter eggs as well! The only thing I'd recommend is to not buy all the dlc unless you want it all. Because most of the dlc you don't need to have a solid experience.

review: If you love syfi, strategy and an amazing track of music this is the game for you. The mechanics aren't the best, but really good compared to other paradox games and other syfi strategy games. I like how certain techs can you give boost, but can be very dangerous if not handled properly and the same with the in game crises. The game has so much flavor in a way that is very open to do a lot things smoothly without a lot of clunk. The clunk that is there can be ignored by the amazing soundtrack that keeps me, and probably others, addicted to the game. I will say the only problem I have with the game is that most of the flavor comes from the add-ons so if you don't have them then you probably won't have as much fun, and there times the add-ons are not that great. Overall this game keeps getting better and better over time, with better playable options/flavors over time.

review:
Paradox doit changer de politique DLC, potentiel gaché, le fun est fini, 10 ans que le jeu est sorti... Son confrère Heart of iron bénéficie de la même politique de pince, ne parlons pas de City Skyline II...

Paradox se prend une culotté par rapport à distant world 2 sur : la personnalisation des vaisseaux, les technologies, l'économie civile (inexistante dans stellaris), l'immersion avec des vaisseaux de frets civils, de transports, des tankers (qui sont d'ailleurs personnalisables tout comme les chasseurs et les bombardiers). Le système de combat terrestre est à des années lumières par rapport à stellaris avec une phase d'approche des troupes au sol conditionnées par les défenses, des drop pods etc. Dans la même vibe les combats spaciaux sont bien plus palpitants. Alors oui distant World est pas facile à prendre en main et sur certain aspect c'est pas ergonomique du tout mais in game c'est un vrai bac à sable, là où le bac à sable de Paradox se situe en amont dans la création de races et civics. C'est vraiment dommage pour un aussi gros studio de proposer un jeu à ce point arcade in game. Game qui se ressembleront beaucoup, en tout cas vous utiliserez toujours les mêmes leviers pour gagner et serez toujours frustrés par les mêmes mécaniques de difficulté très artificielle.

Stellaris n'est pas un mauvais jeu en soi, vous prendrez du plaisir à jouer, mais il pourrait être 10 000 fois mieux. Si seulement le studio se préoccupait à autre chose que de sortir des DLC à moitié vides et 2 fois trop chers. Pour un jeu de 2016 au total si on veut l'expérience complète, il faudra débourser pas moins de 425.99 euros... Un point sur lequel stellaris est excellent c'est les graphismes.

Its colleague Heart of Iron benefits from the same clamp policy, let's not talk about City Skyline II... Paradox is taking a bold step compared to distant world 2 on the customization of ships, the technologies, the civilian economy (non-existent in stellaris), the immersion with civilian cargo ships, transports, tankers (which are also customizable everything like fighters and bombers). The land combat system is light years ahead of stellaris with an approach phase for ground troops conditioned by defenses, drop pods, etc. In the same vibe, the space battles are much more thrilling. So yes Remote World is not easy to handle and in certain aspects it is not ergonomic at all but in game it is a real sandbox, where the Paradox sandbox is located upstream in the creation of races and civics. It's a real shame for such a big studio to offer a game that's so arcade in game. Games that will be very similar, in any case you will always use the same levers to win and will always be frustrated by the same mechanics of very artificial difficulty. Stellaris isn't a bad game in itself, you'll enjoy playing it, but it could be 10,000 times better. If only the studio cared about something other than releasing half-empty and twice-overpriced DLC. For a game from 2016 in total, if you want the full experience, you will have to pay no less than 425.99 euros... One point on which stellaris is excellent is the graphics.

review:
I've had this game for years and with a heavy heart, i have to give it a negative review. There are lots of things, but here's an example. 

I want to like this game. In a way, i do, because I play it of course. But there's always something small and irritating in Paradox games that is so game breaking that it makes you either quit, start over, or uninstall. These are frustrating to me because you can't get achievements unless it's on ironman and you can't reload saves on Ironman. I'm also not referring to save scumming, I'm talking about reloading when some actual BS happens.

I'll give you a few small examples from the other games in case you play so you know where my head's at. 

In hoi4, there's always a situation that if you're playing historical, you remilitarize the Rhineland and occasionally Europe will oppose it so you're either at war at the beginning of the game with half an army, or you start over. 

In CK3, you finally save up 500 gold to form a kingdom, and then your God-stat heir is kidnapped and you have to  pay 499 gold for his ransom. 

Well here, you either start out right next to a hostile race that goes to war with you 5 years after making contact, or your in the middle of your cozy empire next to all of your allies that start crossing your borders and building on the other side of your claimed systems. Or that scientist that you JUST HIRED flies into an uncharted system and immediately gets one shotted by something hostile. Or your special project you're supposed to do is in an empire that has closed it's borders to you so you can't even get the mission out of your log. You will begin immediately declining the habitale worlds survey missions because you will always get to 7/8 planets and then the whole galaxy has been uncovered and there are no more systems to survey.

The point I'm really trying to make is that I've noticed with Paradox games that there always has to be something annoying. There always has to be something going great, then your ally declares war on someone on the other side of the world/galaxy which inadvertently brings you into a war with your neighbor that just so happens to be on the border that you made absolutely no defenses. 

Again, mentioning the ironman/achievement thing, I think that the thing I'm starting to hate about it is that there can be hours of buildup, and then one minor thing or setback can ruin your entire campaign and make you start over. 

Their games are not designed for you to win or lose, just to perpetually restart. Not completion, but engagement farming.

review: Stellaris is maddening---very good about coming up with new content, but not so great at making sure new ideas do not clash with old ones.   Latest example of this appears to be ship size rebalancing---essentially all the ships seem to be 5x size of old ones, with same fleet caps.  If everything had altered to this scale, might be a workable idea.  Alas, Stellaris development team did not appear to rebalance the bad guys! The Great Khan fleets are still the same freaking size/power they were before!  And all of my fleets have 20% of the ships (and 20% of the firepower) we did before.  I like a challenge, but this is idiotic (and obviously a Great Khan floor cleaning ensued).   You broke me.  I'm putting this game in layway until I see a steady stream of reviews saying Paradox fixed it.

review:
I was admittedly turned-off by the absolutely overwhelming nature of the game when I first launched it. I tried, very hard, to grasp the totality of what I was seeing - even going so far as to watch a 'beginner guide' video or two. I put it all down.

Fast forward to 4.3 and I fired it up again. This time I slowed the time to as slow as I could and watched several targeted videos about mechanics and systems as I encountered them. Then it clicked.

My first impression was that this was simply Civ VI in space, which I was totally cool with, especially because it was in real time and not turn-based. Now I see it for what it is: an incredibly deep, fluid and active story of your spaceborne civilization. And I'm in love. It makes other civilization games, at least to me, seem bland and topical compared to this masterpiece. I don't even have any DLC. 

For the record, the one thing I loathe about how this game was further developed and added onto is the endless march of DLC 'content'. I understand to a point some monetization of added content and continued development, but this is true Paradox-style monetization right here. $450 for all the DLC? Pass. It's sad because I would have liked to enjoy more of what the game has to offer.

I'm not far in. Still a fledgling startup yearning for the stars. But I can honestly say I have thoroughly enjoyed my experience thus far and look forward to more. The game is currently on a good sale (~$10 USD) and I would whole-heartedly recommend it to anyone looking for a game that sees you take a civilization from a homeworld to a galactic empire.

My advice at this point: Take it slow, watch some good (and current) videos, and make the story your own. Will update down the road. Oh, and do NOT buy the DLC until you have thoroughly played-through and understand the base game and what you may possibly want out of any DLC. Given that there's so much DLC and the costs are astronomical for it, I'd say maybe the sub is a smarter use of those dollars unless you're going to play this non-stop for 4-5 years.

review:
I've played this game a lot. I am a huge fan. I have sat at my PC as my entire day passes me by, unknown to me, as I mess around with my digital space empire.

But I'm tired. I'm tired of all the drastic changes this game keeps undergoing. They keep reinventing every system of the game again and again. You need to relearn how to play it. Over and over. And ♥♥♥♥ keeps getting buggier and buggier or more and more broken. DLCs are expensive and filled with power creep.

Long term for a support for a game can be nice. Additions can be nice. Fixes can be nice. That isn't the case here. They keep reinventing the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ wheel. The way I used to play the game is no longer even possible. And while that might be fine if the devs were on the ball, they aren't. They keep breaking stuff. 4.0 broke the CPU AI. The computer lost the ability to build an economy. They never fixed that. They just keep pumping out DLCs. They did nerf the economy into the ground for the players though. I keep checking back every month, wondering if things are stable and fun yet. They never are.

I'm sure the devs work hard. I'm sure they could get their house in order if their bosses would let them spend time polishing rather than pumping out DLC. But, that doesn't make the problems that are in the game less egregious.

Но когда ты заморачиваешься со своими фракциями у тебя их 3 при этом если ты начнёшь давить 1\2 а продвигать другую, готовся у тебя просядет стабильность при чём сильно и вот ты няньчишся с этими нетакусями и потом бац разом выскакивает 5!!!! 5 КАРЛ фракций с требованиями что ну нереально выполнить! от слова ни как и твоя стабильность проламывает дно и мчится глубже. Ну можно сказать что мир тюрьма тебе в помощь но есть такооооое маленькое НО, оно не выпадает воооооооооообще, курортный мир пожалуйста легко на бери он постоянно выпадает.  Теперь ещё с флотами весело, не ну с империями у нас положение равное а вот кризисы теперь имбуют и если тебе удасться настроить тонкий баланс не забывай что может подойти рандом и выебать тебя по полной! А и всё что выше написано это про обычные империи ведь играть за единый разум живой\машинный в разы проще! баланс? не ♥♥♥♥♥ его! В игре теперь просто душнилово с микроменеджментом. review_english: Balance? no, we haven’t heard... if earlier random events and research could give you a problem, now it’s just stifling, more than once, research on the basic improvement of the mountain rural energy industry fell under the end game... did you get lost there??? Okay, this is tolerable, you can get it out by trade. But when you bother with your factions, you have 3 of them, and if you start putting pressure on 1 2 and promoting another, I’m ready for your stability to drop significantly, and so you babysit these non-takus and then bam, 5 pops up at once!!!! 5 CARL factions with demands that are unrealistic to fulfill! from the word no matter how your stability breaks through the bottom and rushes deeper. Well, you can say that the world of prison will help you, but there is such a small BUT, it doesn’t fall out at all, the resort world please take it easy, it constantly falls out. Now it’s still fun with fleets, well, with empires, we have an equal position, but crises are now imminent, and if you manage to adjust the delicate balance, don’t forget that a random person can come up and fuck you completely! And everything that is written above is about ordinary empires, because playing for a single living machine mind is much easier! balance? not him! The game is now just stifling with micromanagement. A game that used to bring a lot of positive emotions now brings mostly negative ones... If you are a new player, run, forget that you saw it, you don’t need it, no, well, if you’re a masochist, you’ll probably come in.

review:
Stellaris is a great game. The issue I have is the DLC. Previously, the DLC would go in sale for fair prices in bundles. At some point, Paradox increased prices and changed how the dlc is sold. Now there's no good way to buy it.they have seasons dlc collections, but they disappear after the season ends.

Honestly this makes no sense. As someone that would like to play the game, expanding my knowledge and expanding the features, having old seasons be available for purchase as bundles makes perfect sense. I can buy one season, play for a while, then the next, etc. Instead I just have a messy list of whatever , a bunch or random packs, the whole dlc at once for €300, or a subscription. None of these are good options, and all act as deterrents.

You know what I've chosen? Not to play stellaris.

review:
【初心者には敷居が高すぎて一切お勧めできない】

長く続いているゲームなのにUIが劣悪な為、Modが必須なのに、最近のアプデでゲーム開始前に【実績が解除されるか分からなくなった】りとまるで進歩していない。(４桁で判別できるが外す意味が分からない)
一方、アプデする度に定石だった事＝プレイヤーに有利な【ありとあらゆる要素】が現在進行形で弱体され続けており、過去のプレイ動画を参考しようにも、まるで役に立たない。
Wikiで情報を確認しようにも、大半が過去verのままであり、DLCのレビューも少ないので既に大半のプレイヤーに飽きられていると言って良い。
例えば、ある探索で手に入った研究を行ってしまうと惑星のPOPが全員怪物になってしまう超絶罠イベントが有るのだが、何と五月上旬に日本のWikiで調べても一件もヒットせず、AIに聞いてやっと分かったレベルだった。
幾らなんでもプレイしてる人少な過ぎだろうと笑ってしまった。
(でもAIもAIで誤情報も多く、verが古かったり実績が解除されるModを平気で"解除されません"と紹介してくるポンコツっぷり)

久し振りにプレイしてみても、未だに居住性の低い惑星にPOPが勝手に移住するのがデフォルトかつ、有効な設定ができなかったりと、製作者側がプレイヤー側の利便性を全く考えずに、好き勝手に作ってるんだなってのが伝わって来て悲しい限り。(没落帝国かな？)
その為、購入が必須と言える高額なDLCまで買って遊ぶ価値はまず無いので、他の新しくて安めのゲームを色々と買った方が遥かに有意義だろう。
どうしても遊びたい場合は、バニラに大型Modを入れるのを推奨する。

review_english: It's too difficult for beginners, so I can't recommend it at all.It's a game that's been running for a long time, but the UI is poor, so mods are required, but with the recent update, you can't tell if the achievement will be unlocked before you start the game, so it's not progressing at all (You can tell by the 4 digits, but I don't see the point in removing it.)On the other hand, every time there's an update, all kinds of elements that are advantageous to the player are being weakened, and even if you try to refer to past gameplay videos. Even if you try to check the information on the Wiki, it's completely useless.Most of the information is still in the past version, and there are few reviews of the DLC, so it's safe to say that most players are already tired of it.For example, there is a super trap event where all the POPs on the planet turn into monsters if you conduct research obtained from a certain exploration, but when I looked it up on the Japanese Wiki in early May, I couldn't find any results, and I only found out after asking the AI.I laughed because I thought there were too few people playing it. (However, there is a lot of incorrect information about AI and AI, and there are a lot of idiots who casually introduce mods that are outdated or that unlock achievements as "not unlocked") Even after playing the game for the first time in a while, it's still the default for pops to migrate to planets with low habitability, and it's impossible to set an effective setting.It's sad to see that the creators are making whatever they want without considering the convenience of the player at all (Maybe Fallen Empire) For this reason, it is hardly worth buying and playing the expensive DLC that is required, so it would be much more meaningful to buy a variety of other new and cheaper games.If you really want to play, we recommend installing major mods in vanilla.

review:
I used to really enjoy the game when I bought it back in 2018, but the game has drastically changed since (and multiple times on multiple fronts too).

At first I was okay just relearning how to play the game, but after learning multiple things this year I just cannot recommend buying the game anymore. Sadly I didn't buy it through Steam but through Humble, so I can't request a refund, but I will at least state some of my problems with the game now.

The developers have started including AI generated content in their game, which as an artist I absolutely despise. Besides that the game is bug ridden and like I mentioned before keeps making many changes to both the UI and gameplay, both can be very confusing to deal with, especially with a huge and already bloated game. I also don't like some of the privacy issues I've heard going around, I'd rather not have Paradox collect and sell my information to third parties.

review: Могла бы выйти отличная игра, НО разработчики забили на оптимизацию, более того, решили её испортить. Даже лимит флота игроку уменьшили, но боты оказались не в курсе обновы и того, что корветы нынче занимают 5 мест ради... оптимизации? Которой как не было, так и нет. Ладно, может быть можно просто играть на первой скорости без рассинхронов в мультиплеере? Неа - они будут. Тенденция наметилась ещё с переработки админ-лимита. Раньше можно было красить карту и "сноуболлиться" сколько хочешь - главное, строй административные центры. Но их изменили: лимиты теперь не поднимешь, нужно дружить с соседями! Захватывать? Бред какой-то. Боты захватят все планеты с 20% пригодности, засрут карту хабитатами, но если ты игрок - будь добр, прыгни в канаву и получи штрафы за размер империи. Играй через вассалов, чтобы... да. Итого на текущий момент: имеем два DLC, ломающих синхронизацию; флоты, "убитые в пиво", чтобы какой-нибудь экстерминатус или рой, респавнящийся в двух секторах от столицы, наверняка тебя сожрал, имея 9к мощи против твоих 1к, станции на 2к с полностью забитым лимитом. Короче, я к чему: если всё же хочется сыграть в это, ставьте версию 4.2.4 и не берите DLC на биокораблики, штормы и "лаво-челов" - они ломают синхру

review_english: It could have been a great game, BUT the developers neglected optimization, moreover, they decided to ruin it. They even reduced the player’s fleet limit, but the bots were not aware of the update and the fact that corvettes now occupy 5 places for the sake of... optimization? Which both did not exist and still does not exist. Okay, maybe you can just play at first speed without out of sync in multiplayer? Nope - they will. The trend has emerged since the reworking of the admin limit. Previously, you could color the map and “snowball” as much as you wanted - the main thing was to build administrative centers. But they changed the limits, now you can’t raise them, you need to be friends with your neighbors! Grab? Some kind of nonsense. Bots will capture all planets with 20 suitability, litter the map with habitats, but if you are a player, be kind enough to jump into a ditch and receive penalties for the size of your empire. Play through vassals to... yeah. In total, at the moment we have two DLCs that break the synchronization of fleets, “killed in beer”, so that some exterminatus or swarm, respawning in two sectors from the capital, will probably devour you, having 9k power against your 1k, stations at 2k with a completely filled limit. In short, what am I saying, if you still want to play this, install version 4.2.4 and don’t take the DLC for bioships, storms and “lavo-men” - they break the synchronization

review:
在360亿光年的尺度下，也许从没有什么真正壮丽的乐土或宏大热血的战争。但在那个室女座星团本星系群的角落里，无数的国家与文明在那刻下带着征服正义血债恐惧和谐危机存亡联合背叛贪婪蔑视天灾的名为“银河”的丰碑，却并非功绩而是坟墓。像那些曾经掌握太虚科技的天机工程与焚天利器到随意更改宇宙常数、跨越维度感知灵能却未能成神?的“前文明”那样，众文明互帮互助尔虞我诈、齐心协力相互算计着把宇宙中这颗尘埃推向万丈深渊。而你是一个事件之外的记录者，看着虫子们自谕文明却兴肆意毁灭文明之事却并不阻止，而是饶有兴致的欣赏、品味甚至帮助虫子们从万骨堆中扶正成神。帮凶、刽子手、极恶行刑人
不，这都不对，因为这就是

review:
Stellaris to gra, która nie zaczyna się od wielkiego wybuchu akcji. Ona zaczyna się od ciszy. Od jednej planety. Od kilku statków zwiadowczych. I od tej charakterystycznej myśli: „A co, jeśli to właśnie moje imperium zapisze się w historii galaktyki?”.
I to jest moment, w którym Stellaris zaczyna działać — powoli, ale z ogromną siłą.

To, co najbardziej mi się podoba, to poczucie skali. Nie budujesz tu miasta, państwa ani kontynentu. Budujesz cywilizację, która ma swoje prawa, kulturę, filozofię i ambicje. Każda decyzja — od ustroju, przez etykę, po sposób prowadzenia badań — zmienia sposób, w jaki Twoje imperium funkcjonuje. I to nie są kosmetyczne różnice. To są wybory, które naprawdę wpływają na rozgrywkę.

Eksploracja to czysta przyjemność. Każdy nowy układ gwiezdny to mała historia. Czasem znajdziesz porzucone stacje badawcze, czasem starożytne ruiny, a czasem coś, czego lepiej było nie budzić. Stellaris potrafi zaskoczyć w sposób, który sprawia, że chcesz odkrywać dalej, nawet jeśli wiesz, że może to skończyć się katastrofą.

Wojny w tej grze mają swój ciężar. To nie jest RTS, gdzie klikasz i patrzysz, jak jednostki się tłuką. Tu każda flota to miesiące pracy, badań i surowców. Każda bitwa to ryzyko. A kiedy dwie potężne armady spotykają się w jednym systemie, czujesz, że naprawdę toczy się walka o przyszłość galaktyki.

I wreszcie — historie. Stellaris jest maszyną do generowania opowieści.
Możesz stworzyć pokojową federację, która zjednoczy pół galaktyki…
Albo imperium, które zamieni wszystko wokół w swoje kolonie…
Albo kolektyw maszyn, który uzna organiczne życie za błąd ewolucji…

Każda rozgrywka to inna opowieść. I to właśnie sprawia, że ta gra tak bardzo mi się podoba — bo nigdy nie wiesz, dokąd Cię zaprowadzi.

Stellaris to strategia, która nie tylko daje Ci narzędzia. Ona daje Ci galaktykę pełną możliwości. A reszta zależy już tylko od Ciebie.

review_english: Stellaris is a game that doesn't start with a big burst of action. It begins with silence. From one planet. From several scout ships. And from this characteristic thought: What if my empire goes down in the history of the galaxy? . And this is the moment when Stellaris begins to act slowly but with great force. What I like most is the sense of scale. You are not building a city, a country or a continent here. You are building a civilization that has its own laws, culture, philosophy and ambitions. Every decision, from political system to ethics to the way you conduct research, changes the way your empire functions. And these are not cosmetic differences. These are the choices that really impact the gameplay. Exploration is pure pleasure. Each new star system tells a little story. Sometimes you'll find abandoned research stations, sometimes ancient ruins, and sometimes something you'd better not wake up. Stellaris can surprise you in a way that makes you want to keep exploring, even if you know it could end in disaster. The wars in this game have their weight. This isn't an RTS where you click and watch units smash. Here, each fleet takes months of work, research and raw materials. Every battle is a risk. And when two powerful armadas meet in one system, you feel that the future of the galaxy is truly being fought for. And finally, stories. Stellaris is a story generating machine. You can create a peaceful federation that will unite half the galaxy, or an empire that will turn everything around into its colonies, or a collective of machines that will consider organic life a mistake of evolution. Each game is a different story. And that's what makes me like this game so much because you never know where it will take you. Stellaris is a strategy that doesn't just give you tools. She gives you a galaxy full of possibilities. And the rest depends only on you.

review:
Es ist zwar außerhalb von Rabattaktionen teilweise echt teuer, aber ich kann Stellaris absolut empfehlen.
Ich habe mir das Grundspiel damals im Rabatt für etwa 10€ geholt, inklusive Utopia und zwei weiteren Erweiterungen, und allein damit hatte ich schon extrem viel Spaß.

Mittlerweile habe ich mir noch mehrere DLCs wie Stellaris: Federations, Stellaris: Apocalypse, Stellaris: Machine Age sowie ein paar Story Packs geholt. Das Lustige daran ist: Ich habe bisher noch nicht einmal wirklich angefangen, die ganzen DLC-Inhalte aktiv zu spielen, und trotzdem fühlt sich das Spiel jetzt schon unglaublich umfangreich und lebendig an.

Vor allem DLCs wie Federations oder Apocalypse erweitern das Spielerlebnis extrem durch mehr Möglichkeiten, mehr Variation und einfach mehr „galaktisches Chaos“. Die Story Packs wiederum machen die Galaxie deutlich lebendiger und sorgen dafür, dass überall irgendetwas Interessantes passiert.

Das Spiel kann am Anfang etwas überwältigend wirken, aber sobald man die grundlegenden Systeme versteht, entwickelt es sich zu einem dieser Spiele, bei denen man plötzlich merkt, dass schon wieder mehrere Stunden vergangen sind.

Mein persönlicher Tipp wäre aber definitiv: Im Sale kaufen. Dann bekommt man für vergleichsweise wenig Geld absurd viel Content und eines der besten Sci-Fi-Strategie-Spiele überhaupt.

Ich würde außerdem empfehlen, die erste Runde erstmal komplett ohne zusätzliche DLCs zu spielen, da das Grundspiel schon genug Inhalt bietet und man so die wichtigsten Systeme deutlich entspannter lernen kann. Vieles wirkt am Anfang kompliziert, ist später aber eigentlich ziemlich logisch, und für die typischen Anfängerfehler oder Grundmechaniken findet man online auch schnell kurze 5-Minuten-Videos mit hilfreichen Tipps.

review_english: It's sometimes really expensive outside of discount campaigns, but I can absolutely recommend Stellaris. I got the base game at a discount for around €10, including Utopia and two other expansions, and I had a lot of fun with that alone. I have now gotten several more DLCs such as Stellaris Federations, Stellaris Apocalypse, Stellaris Machine Age and a few story packs. The funny thing is, I haven't even really started actively playing all of the DLC content yet, and yet the game already feels incredibly expansive and alive. DLCs ​​like Federations or Apocalypse in particular expand the gaming experience extremely with more options, more variation and simply more galactic chaos. The story packs, in turn, make the galaxy much more lively and ensure that something interesting happens everywhere. The game can seem a bit overwhelming at first, but once you understand the basic systems, it becomes one of those games where you suddenly realize that several hours have passed again. My personal tip would definitely be to buy on sale. Then you get an absurd amount of content and one of the best sci-fi strategy games ever for comparatively little money. I would also recommend playing the first round completely without additional DLCs, as the base game already offers enough content and you can learn the most important systems much more relaxed. A lot of things seem complicated at the beginning, but are actually quite logical later on, and you can quickly find short 5-minute videos online with helpful tips for the typical beginner mistakes or basic mechanics.

review:
I hate this game's UI. I've tried different mods. They obviously want you sitting in front of a monitor playing this game. It's very unfriendly for people who like to relax on a couch and play this game. The fonts are so small. I've tried changing resolutions down to 720 to make things bigger and it doesn't really help. I tried adding different mods. Even on Crusader Kings 3 a person made a mod that made things bigger without having buttons and icons hidden off screen. It's just so annoying it angers me. 

Can't even play this game cause of the UI. I've tried coming back year after year to see if anything has changed. Nope... Wasted my money. I only have 15 hours of play and you can guess what that time was spent on, in the game's setting, and having to constantly bounce back and forth going into documents to redo the settings/txt fild to change the gui_scale from different numbers to no avail. You'd think after spending millions of dollars on a game like this, they'd make some sort of settings for people who don't wanna sit hunched over in front of a monitor to play this. I usually sit 8 feet away on a nice comfy chair playing games at 120 fps on 1920x 1080.

I'M DONE!
